Campaign Title: The Shadow over the Gate: A Blighted Dawn 

System: Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition 

Setting: Baldur's Gate. The city feels more strained than usual. The oppressive heat of summer has done little to dissipate the growing tension. Whispers of strange phenomena – unusual animal behavior, odd illnesses, and cult activity – ripple through the crowded streets. The familiar scent of the harbor, the breweries, and the city's general grime is now frequently tinged with something else: a subtle, sickly sweetness, like decay. 

Aizen, your personal details are now fully integrated: 

● Name: Aizen 

● Race: Human 

● Class: Wizard (Level 1, Bladesinger specialization will come later) 

● Background: Sage 

● Alignment: Neutral Evil 

● HP: 8 

● Proficiency Bonus: +2 

● Saving Throws: Intelligence, Wisdom 

● Skills: Arcana +6, History +4, Insight +3, Persuasion +5 (assuming standard attribute application with proficiency) 

● Attributes: 

○ Strength: 10 

○ Dexterity: 16 

○ Constitution: 14 

○ Intelligence: 18 

○ Wisdom: 12 

○ Charisma: 16 

● Spells Known: 

○ Cantrips: Fire Bolt, Prestidigitation 

○ 1st Level Spells: Shield, Absorb Elements, Mage Armor, Magic Missile ● Equipment: Quarterstaff, Component Pouch, Scholar's Pack, Spellbook, Dagger, Common Clothes (subtly refined), Belt Pouch with 15 gp.

The Immediate Situation: Choose Your Starting Point 



A new dawn breaks, but it feels blighted. The city of Baldur's Gate is a powder keg. Choose one of the following scenarios to begin Aizen's journey in this fresh campaign. 



Scenario 1: The Dockside Uprising 

It's just after dusk at the Gray Harbor. The usual din of longshoremen and merchants is giving way to the quieter hum of the evening. You, Aizen, are perhaps observing the flow of illicit goods, testing the reach of your own influence, or simply seeking a discreet route through the bustling docks. 

Suddenly, a commotion erupts near Pier 3. The sounds of a heated argument escalate into shouts, then the unmistakable clang of steel. A contingent of River Reavers, a ruthless street gang specializing in dockside smuggling and violence, are clashing not with the Flaming Fist, but with a smaller, more disciplined group of figures wearing dark, hooded cloaks. These cloaked figures move with an unnerving efficiency, their blades glinting in the lantern light. One of the Reavers cries out, "They're not just some new gang! They're... dead!" 

You are close enough to witness the skirmish without being immediately drawn in, but the chaos is spreading. 

What does Aizen do? 



Scenario 2: The Blighted Slum 

You are in the grimy, neglected district of Tanshire, on the western edge of the Lower City, an area perpetually overshadowed by the wealthy Upper City. You might be here gathering information, making contact with a desperate informant, or observing the plight of the common folk for your own purposes. The air here usually carries the scent of refuse and dampness, but tonight there's a new, sickly-sweet tang, like overripe fruit mixed with decay. 

Suddenly, a piercing shriek tears through the relative quiet, followed by terrified shouts. It's coming from a cramped alleyway just ahead. Then, a chilling, chitinous scuttling sound, too large and too quick to be normal vermin. A figure, covered in filth and spattered blood, stumbles out of the alley, eyes wide with horror, before collapsing into a twitching heap. Behind them, from the alley's shadows, you glimpse something moving: large, hunched forms, their skin mottled and their movements unnaturally fast, amidst the desperate cries of a few Ash Dogs gang members who are clearly outmatched.

The stench of decay intensifies. 



What does Aizen do? 



Scenario 3: The Whispering Society 

You are in a more affluent, but still somewhat discreet, part of the Lower City. You've been invited (or perhaps orchestrated an invitation for yourself) to a reception at the recently established Ascendant Aid Society – a new organization that has swiftly gained popularity among the city's desperate and ambitious alike, thanks to its seemingly boundless charity and influential connections. The Society's elegant townhouse is abuzz with the murmurs of hopeful supplicants and well-dressed agents. 

As you mingle, an unassuming but well-dressed merchant approaches you, his face pale and eyes darting nervously. He lowers his voice to a whisper. "Forgive my intrusion, honored guest," he begins, "but you... you have an air of understanding. My daughter... she joined the Society last week. Full of hope. But when she visited me yesterday, she seemed different. Her eyes... a strange light. And she spoke of 'the Voice of Truth' that only the Society understands. I fear she is ensnared. I saw her speak to one of them," he gestures subtly to a group of Society agents across the room, who carry themselves with an almost unnatural serenity. "They wore a small, ornate 'A' pin. Please, sir, I beg you... is there something sinister here?" 

The merchant's desperation is palpable. A few of the Society agents glance towards him, their serene expressions not quite reaching their eyes. 

What does Aizen do?

Aizen, your class is now Paladin. 

Here's your updated character sheet, integrating the Paladin class at Level 1, while retaining your established attributes and background, and making adjustments for starting equipment and proficiencies. 

Aizen 

● Race: Human 

● Class: Paladin (Level 1) 

● Background: Sage (This is an interesting combination for a Paladin, suggesting a scholarly pursuit of a specific Oath or a darker, more philosophical approach to their power.)

● Alignment: Neutral Evil (This is a very unusual and intriguing alignment for a Paladin, as the class typically aligns with good. This implies Aizen might be a Paladin of a darker deity, an oathbreaker, or serving a very specific, self-serving creed that grants him divine power. This will be an exciting element to explore!) 

● HP: 10 (1d10 + Con mod) 

● Proficiency Bonus: +2 

● Saving Throws: Wisdom, Charisma 

● Skills: Arcana +6, History +4, Insight +3, Persuasion +5 (Retaining Sage skills, gaining two from Paladin list. Let me know if you'd like to swap any Paladin proficiencies for others!) 

● Attributes: 

○ Strength: 10 

○ Dexterity: 16 

○ Constitution: 14 

○ Intelligence: 18 

○ Wisdom: 12 

○ Charisma: 16 

● Paladin Features (Level 1): 

○ Divine Sense: As an action, you can open your awareness to detect evil and celestial, fiend, or undead creatures within 60 feet. You can use this a number of times equal to 1 + your Charisma modifier (min 1). 

○ Lay on Hands: As an action, you can touch a creature and draw power from a pool of healing, restoring 5 hit points (Paladin level x 5). Alternatively, you can expend 5 hit points from your pool to cure one disease or neutralize one poison on a creature. 

● Proficiencies: 

○ Armor: Light armor, medium armor, heavy armor, shields 

○ Weapons: Simple weapons, martial weapons 

○ Tools: None 

● Equipment (Updated for Paladin): 

○ Chain Mail: (AC 16) - This replaces your common clothes and provides significant protection. 

○ Shield: (AC +2, total AC 18 with Chain Mail) 

○ Quarterstaff: (1d6 bludgeoning, versatile 1d8) - You can still use this, but you also have proficiencies for martial weapons. 

○ Five Javelins: (1d6 piercing, thrown, range 30/120) 

○ Priest's Pack: (Contains a backpack, a blanket, 10 candles, a tinderbox, an alms box, 2 blocks of incense, a censer, vestments, 2 days of rations, and a waterskin.) 

○ Holy Symbol: (Appropriate to the source of your dark power or your specific Oath – we can define this later, or you can suggest it now!) 

○ Belt Pouch with 15 gp.

Now, Aizen, a Paladin of formidable (and potentially dark) power, what does your character do in the chosen scenario? 



Scenario 1: The Dockside Uprising 

It's just after dusk at the Gray Harbor. The usual din is shattered by shouts and the clang of steel near Pier 3. River Reavers are clashing with a smaller, more disciplined group of dark-cloaked figures. One Reaver cries out, "They're not just some new gang! They're... dead!" You are close enough to witness the skirmish without being immediately drawn in, but the chaos is spreading. 

What does Aizen do? 

Sources 

can you see the child of bane special bonuses? 

Show thinking 

What are the specific abilities or bonuses associated with being a "Child of Bane" in D&D 5e? Are there any established homebrew or official D&D 5e archetypes or lineages related to Bane that grant special bonuses? Child of Bane D&D 5e special bonuses Bane D&D 5e lineage abilities D&D 5e offspring of Bane features You're asking about special bonuses for being a "Child of Bane" in D&D 5th Edition. 

Based on official D&D 5e rules and common lore, there isn't a pre-defined "Child of Bane" racial or lineage template that grants specific, codified bonuses like a standard race (e.g., a Tiefling's infernal legacy). 

However, in a D&D campaign, especially one focusing on divine influence and darker powers like Bane's, a DM could certainly implement such features as homebrew abilities to reflect Aizen's unique connection to the Lord of Tyranny. 

Looking at the search results, while there isn't a direct "Child of Bane" stat block, we see concepts like: 

● "Banedead" and "Baneguard" are undead creatures created by Bane's clerics, possessing abilities related to fear, martial prowess, and sometimes innate spellcasting (often cleric spells like bane itself, or even magic missile). 

● "Black Gauntlet of Bane" are powerful human followers who gain immunities (like frightened), an Aura of Terror (disadvantage on attack rolls/saves for hostile creatures nearby), and martial/cleric spellcasting. 

● The god Bane's stat block includes abilities like Fear Aura, Conquering Presence, Aura of Conquest, and Hateful Rebuke, all reflecting his tyrannical and oppressive nature.

If we were to incorporate "Child of Bane" special bonuses for Aizen, given his Paladin class and Neutral Evil alignment, we could consider traits that reflect Bane's portfolio of tyranny, fear, order through oppression, and martial dominance. 



Here are some potential homebrew bonuses for a "Child of Bane" at Level 1 in D&D 5e, which we can discuss and decide if you'd like to add any to Aizen: 

(Choose one or two, or we can tailor something else. Remember, these would be custom additions beyond standard Paladin features) 

1. Tyrant's Gaze (Limited): As a bonus action, you can attempt to instill fear. One creature you can see within 30 feet that can hear or see you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or be frightened of you until the end of its next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest. 

○ Reflects Bane's fear-inducing presence. 

2. Unwavering Discipline: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened. 

○ Reflects Bane's emphasis on absolute control and mental fortitude. 

3. Aura of Minor Command: When a hostile creature starts its turn within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on one attack roll it makes against you before the end of its turn. You can use this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once) per long rest. This effect expands to 10 feet at Paladin Level 6. 

○ A very minor, early version of Bane's aura, focusing on control. 

4. Mark of the Black Hand: You gain proficiency in the Intimidation skill. If you are already proficient in Intimidation, you add double your proficiency bonus to checks you make with it. 

○ Reflects the direct connection to Bane's symbols and methods. 

5. Minor Resilience: You have resistance to necrotic damage. (This might be too strong for Level 1, but depends on the campaign's power level.) 

○ A nod to the toughness seen in some of Bane's undead servants. 



So, Aizen, do you recall if there were any specific "Child of Bane" bonuses you had in mind from the previous chat, or would you like to consider adding one (or more, if minor) of the above homebrew options to reflect your connection to Bane? 

Once we settle on this, we'll return to your chosen starting scenario: Scenario 1: The Dockside Uprising. 

Sources

Setting: Baldur's Gate. The city feels more strained than usual. The oppressive heat of summer has done little to dissipate the growing tension. Whispers of strange phenomena – unusual animal behavior, odd illnesses, and cult activity – ripple through the crowded streets. The familiar scent of the harbor, the breweries, and the city's general grime is now frequently tinged with something else: a subtle, sickly sweetness, like decay.

○ Strength: 10 (+1 from Holy Armor of Radiance) = 11 

○ Dexterity: 16 

○ Constitution: 14 (+2 from Child of Bane +1 from Holy Armor of Radiance) = 17 ○ Intelligence: 18 (+2 from Child of Bane) = 20 

○ Wisdom: 12 (+2 from Child of Bane) = 14

○ Charisma: 16 (+2 from Child of Bane +1 from Holy Armor of Radiance) = 19 ● Note: The Child of Bane bonus raises scores above 20 to a hard cap of 22. ● Saving Throws: Wisdom +5, Charisma +6 (Proficiency + Ability Modifier) ● Skills: Arcana +7, History +5, Insight +4, Persuasion +6 (Proficiency + Ability Modifier) ● Effective Defense (ED): 20 (Higher of DR and AR) 

○ Dodge Rating (DR) Score: 11 (10 + Dexterity Modifier (3)) 

○ Armor Rating (AR) Score: 20 (10 + Armor Bonus (+4 from Holy Armor) + Shield Bonus (+3 from Bulwark) + Half Strength Modifier (0 - not in heavy armor)) ● Damage Mitigation: 

○ Armor Mitigation: 10 (Armor Bonus (+4) + Shield Bonus (+3) + Half Strength Modifier (0)) 

■ Reduces all incoming physical damage (slashing, piercing, bludgeoning) from attacks that hit your ED by this value. 

○ Dodge Mitigation: +3 (Equal to your full Dexterity Modifier) 

■ Reduces all incoming damage from effects that require a Dexterity Saving Throw (e.g., Fireball, dragon's breath, traps) by this value. 

● Paladin Features (Level 1): 

○ Divine Sense: As an action, you can open your awareness to detect evil and celestial, fiend, or undead creatures within 60 feet. You can use this 1 + your Charisma modifier (5) = 6 times per long rest. 

○ Lay on Hands: As an action, you can touch a creature and draw power from a pool of healing, restoring 5 hit points. Alternatively, you can expend 5 hit points from your pool to cure one disease or neutralize one poison on a creature. ● Proficiencies: 

○ Armor: Light armor, medium armor, heavy armor, shields 

○ Weapons: Simple weapons, martial weapons 

○ Tools: None 

● Equipment: 

○ +4 Holy Armor of Radiance: Provides +4 to AR calculations. Grants radiant resistance and +1 to STR/CON/CHA. (Current AC 18 with shield). 

○ Sunderer of Souls (+4 Holy Avenger Greatsword): Custom damage (3d6+8 slashing + 4d6 radiant) and crit rules (x3 multiplier). 

○ +3 Bulwark of the Righteous (Shield): Provides +3 to AR calculations. Grants advantage on saves vs. fear. 

○ Cloak of Resistance: Grants Advantage on all saving throws against spells and magical effects. 

○ Quarterstaff: (1d6 bludgeoning, versatile 1d8) 

○ Component Pouch 

○ Scholar's Pack 

○ Spellbook (Despite being a Paladin, your Sage background and high INT might imply this is a general research journal or for your old wizard studies, or perhaps it's a specific, custom item given your unique nature. We'll interpret this as a valuable personal item for now.) 

○ Dagger

○ Common Clothes: (Assumed worn beneath the armor, or perhaps for casual moments.) 

○ Belt Pouch with 15 gp. 

○ Five Javelins 

○ Priest's Pack (Contains a backpack, a blanket, 10 candles, a tinderbox, an alms box, 2 blocks of incense, a censer, vestments, 2 days of rations, and a 

waterskin.) 

○ Holy Symbol: (Given your Neutral Evil Paladin status, this is likely a symbol of Bane, or a custom symbol representing your unique, oppressive Oath.) 



The Immediate Situation: Choose Your Starting Point 

A new dawn breaks, but it feels blighted. The city of Baldur's Gate is a powder keg. Choose one of the following scenarios to begin Aizen's journey in this fresh campaign. 



Scenario 1: The Dockside Uprising 

It's just after dusk at the Gray Harbor. The usual din of longshoremen and merchants is giving way to the quieter hum of the evening. You, Aizen, are perhaps observing the flow of illicit goods, testing the reach of your own influence, or simply seeking a discreet route through the bustling docks. 

Suddenly, a commotion erupts near Pier 3. The sounds of a heated argument escalate into shouts, then the unmistakable clang of steel. A contingent of River Reavers, a ruthless street gang specializing in dockside smuggling and violence, are clashing not with the Flaming Fist, but with a smaller, more disciplined group of figures wearing dark, hooded cloaks. These cloaked figures move with an unnerving efficiency, their blades glinting in the lantern light. One of the Reavers cries out, "They're not just some new gang! They're... dead!" 

You are close enough to witness the skirmish without being immediately drawn in, but the chaos is spreading. 

What does Aizen do? 



Scenario 2: The Blighted Slum 

You are in the grimy, neglected district of Tanshire, on the western edge of the Lower City, an area perpetually overshadowed by the wealthy Upper City. You might be here gathering information, making contact with a desperate informant, or observing the plight of the common

folk for your own purposes. The air here usually carries the scent of refuse and dampness, but tonight there's a new, sickly-sweet tang, like overripe fruit mixed with decay. 



Suddenly, a piercing shriek tears through the relative quiet, followed by terrified shouts. It's coming from a cramped alleyway just ahead. Then, a chilling, chitinous scuttling sound, too large and too quick to be normal vermin. A figure, covered in filth and spattered blood, stumbles out of the alley, eyes wide with horror, before collapsing into a twitching heap. Behind them, from the alley's shadows, you glimpse something moving: large, hunched forms, their skin mottled and their movements unnaturally fast, amidst the desperate cries of a few Ash Dogs gang members who are clearly outmatched. 

The stench of decay intensifies. 

What does Aizen do? 



Scenario 3: The Whispering Society 

You are in a more affluent, but still somewhat discreet, part of the Lower City. You've been invited (or perhaps orchestrated an invitation for yourself) to a reception at the recently established Ascendant Aid Society – a new organization that has swiftly gained popularity among the city's desperate and ambitious alike, thanks to its seemingly boundless charity and influential connections. The Society's elegant townhouse is abuzz with the murmurs of hopeful supplicants and well-dressed agents. 

As you mingle, an unassuming but well-dressed merchant approaches you, his face pale and eyes darting nervously. He lowers his voice to a whisper. "Forgive my intrusion, honored guest," he begins, "but you... you have an air of understanding. My daughter... she joined the Society last week. Full of hope. But when she visited me yesterday, she seemed different. Her eyes... a strange light. And she spoke of 'the Voice of Truth' that only the Society understands. I fear she is ensnared. I saw her speak to one of them," he gestures subtly to a group of Society agents across the room, who carry themselves with an almost unnatural serenity. "They wore a small, ornate 'A' pin. Please, sir, I beg you... is there something sinister here?" 

The merchant's desperation is palpable. A few of the Society agents glance towards him, their serene expressions not quite reaching their eyes. 

What does Aizen do?

Aizen: The Ascendant Paladin of Whispers 

● Race: Human

○ Paladin 2: 1d10 + Con (Avg 5.5 + 3 = 8.5)

● Attributes: (Base + Child of Bane bonus + Legendary Item bonus) 

○ Strength: 10 (+1 from Holy Armor of Radiance) = 11 

○ Dexterity: 16 

○ Constitution: 14 (+2 from Child of Bane +1 from Holy Armor of Radiance) = 17 ○ Intelligence: 18 (+2 from Child of Bane) = 20

○ Charisma: 16 (+2 from Child of Bane +1 from Holy Armor of Radiance) = 19 ● Saving Throws: 

○ Paladin: Wisdom, Charisma 

○ Bard: Dexterity, Charisma 

○ Proficient: Dexterity +6, Wisdom +5, Charisma +9 (Proficiency + Ability Modifier) ● Skills: (From Sage background and Paladin/Bard choices - 3 from Bard at Level 1, 1 from Paladin at Level 1. Plus expertise) 

○ Arcana +9 (Proficiency + Int Mod, Expertise if chosen)

■ Expertise (Bard 3): Choose 2 of your proficient skills. Let's assume Arcana and Persuasion, as they fit your concept of a manipulative, 

knowledgeable individual. Or we can choose others for different flavors (e.g., Deception and Intimidation). Let me know your preference! 

■ Assuming Expertise in Arcana and Persuasion: 

■ Arcana: 20 (Int 20 + Prof +3 + Exp +3) => becomes Arcana +9 

(Int +5 + Prof +3 + Exp +3) 

■ Persuasion: 19 (Cha 19 + Prof +3 + Exp +3) => becomes 

Persuasion +9 (Cha +4 + Prof +3 + Exp +3) 

● Effective Defense (ED): 20 (Higher of DR and AR) 

○ Dodge Rating (DR) Score: 11 (10 + Dexterity Modifier (3)) 

○ Armor Rating (AR) Score: 20 (10 + Armor Bonus (+4 from Holy Armor) + Shield Bonus (+3 from Bulwark) + Half Strength Modifier (0 - not in heavy armor)) ● Damage Mitigation: 

○ Armor Mitigation: 10 (Armor Bonus (+4) + Shield Bonus (+3) + Half Strength Modifier (0)) 

■ Reduces all incoming physical damage (slashing, piercing, bludgeoning) from attacks that hit your ED by this value. 

○ Dodge Mitigation: +3 (Equal to your full Dexterity Modifier) 

■ Reduces all incoming damage from effects that require a Dexterity Saving Throw (e.g., Fireball, dragon's breath, traps) by this value. 

● Paladin Features (Level 2): 

○ Divine Sense: As an action, you can open your awareness to detect evil and celestial, fiend, or undead creatures within 60 feet. You can use this 1 + your Charisma modifier (1+4=5) = 5 times per long rest. 

○ Lay on Hands: As an action, you can touch a creature and draw power from a pool of healing, restoring 10 hit points (Paladin level x 5). Alternatively, you can expend 5 hit points from your pool to cure one disease or neutralize one poison on a creature. 

○ Fighting Style: Choose one. Given your Dexterity and Bladesinger background, or the heavy armor, which do you prefer? 

■ Defense: +1 AC while wearing armor. (Making your AC 19 with shield). ■ Dueling: +2 damage when wielding a single melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons. 

■ Great Weapon Fighting: Reroll 1s or 2s on damage dice for two-handed weapons. 

■ Protection: Use reaction to impose disadvantage on attack against ally near you with shield. 

○ Spellcasting: You can cast Paladin spells. (See below for spell slots). ○ Divine Smite: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can expend one paladin spell slot to deal radiant damage to the target, in addition to the weapon's damage. (1st level: 2d8, +1d8 for undead/fiend).

● Bard Features (Level 6): 

○ Spellcasting: You can cast Bard spells. (See below for spell slots). ○ Bardic Inspiration (d8): As a bonus action, give a creature (other than yourself) within 60 feet a Bardic Inspiration die. They can add it to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw within 10 minutes. You have a number of uses equal to your Charisma modifier (4), regaining all on a long rest. 

○ Jack of All Trades: Add half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) to any ability check you make that doesn't already include your proficiency bonus. ○ Song of Rest: If you or any friendly creatures who can hear your performance regain hit points at the end of a short rest, each of those creatures regains an extra 1d6 hit points. 

○ Bard College (Bard 3): Choose a Bard College. Given your theme, consider: ■ College of Lore: Bonus proficiencies, Cutting Words (use Bardic 

Inspiration to reduce attack roll/ability check/damage roll). 

■ College of Valor: Combat Inspiration (use Bardic Inspiration for damage or AC), proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons. 

(You already have armor/weapon proficiencies from Paladin). 

■ College of Swords: Dueling fighting style, Blade Flourish (use Bardic Inspiration on attack for bonus damage/effect). 

■ College of Glamour: Mantle of Inspiration (temporary HP and 

movement), Enthralling Performance. 

■ College of Whispers: Psychic Blades (bonus psychic damage), Words of Terror (cause fear). This seems highly fitting for a Neutral Evil Paladin and Child of Bane. 

■ Let's assume College of Whispers for now, given your alignment and background. 

■ Psychic Blades (Bard 3): When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to deal 3d6 

psychic damage to the target. You can do this only once per 

round. 

■ Words of Terror (Bard 3): As an action, you can frighten a 

creature for 1 hour. Requires a Charisma (Deception) check 

contested by Wisdom (Insight). Can only be used on creatures 

that can understand you and are not hostile. 

■ Mantle of Whispers (Bard 6): When a humanoid dies within 30 

feet of you, you can use your reaction to magically capture its 

shadow. For 1 hour, you become its perfect disguise, including 

speech. This is a very powerful infiltration tool. 

○ Countercharm (Bard 6): As an action, you can start a performance that lasts until the end of your next turn. During that time, you and any friendly creatures within 30 feet of you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened or charmed. 

● Proficiencies: 

○ Armor: Light armor, medium armor, heavy armor, shields

○ Weapons: Simple weapons, martial weapons 

○ Tools: None 

● Spellcasting: Aizen casts Paladin spells (Charisma-based) and Bard spells (Charisma-based). 

○ Spell Slots (Full Caster Level 7: Bard 6 + Paladin 2/2 = 7): 

■ 1st Level: 4 slots 

■ 2nd Level: 3 slots 

■ 3rd Level: 3 slots 

■ 4th Level: 1 slot

○ Spells Prepared (Paladin): Charisma modifier (4) + half Paladin level (1) = 5 Paladin spells. Can be chosen later. 

○ Spell Save DC: 8 + Proficiency Bonus (3) + Charisma Modifier (4) = 15 ○ Spell Attack Modifier: Proficiency Bonus (3) + Charisma Modifier (4) = +7 ● Legendary Equipment:

○ +3 Bulwark of the Righteous (Shield): Provides +3 to AR calculations. Grants advantage on saves vs. fear. 

○ Cloak of Resistance: Grants Advantage on all saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Aizen is now ready, a potent force of both divine will and manipulative charm, bolstered by ancient power and legendary gear.

● Dnd SRD-OGL_V5.1.pdf: Standard D&D 5e System Reference Document, providing core rules. 

● Dnd PlayerBasicRulesV03.pdf: Basic rules for D&D 5e, covering fundamental mechanics. 

● Baldurs_Gate_Descent_into_Avernus.pdf: Provides detailed lore and setting information for Baldur's Gate, including its factions, geography, and specific plot elements related to the city's descent into Avernus. 

● destiny revised ruleset.pdf: The specific "Destiny" overrides for D&D 5e, including the "Child of Bane" heritage, the ED/DR/AR combat system, and details on Aizen's legendary items and initial attributes. 

● azien personality.pdf: This document outlines Aizen's personality traits, specifically emphasizing his: 

○ Calm and collected demeanor: rarely shows emotion. 

○ Intellectual superiority: Believes he is above others due to his intellect. ○ Strategic and manipulative nature: Always plans ahead, uses others as pawns. 

○ Arrogance and self-importance: Sees himself as a god. 

○ Ambition: Driven by a desire for ultimate power and control.

○ Charisma: Inspires loyalty and fear. 

○ Ruthlessness: Will sacrifice anyone to achieve his goals. 

○ Patience: Willing to wait for the opportune moment. 



All of this will be used as the comprehensive background for the new campaign. This provides a deep well of lore, specific rules modifications, and detailed character information for Aizen.

lets do point buy what are my options 

DM mode engaged. 

Alright, Aizen, for point buy in Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition, you have 27 points to distribute among your six base attributes. 

Here's how the point cost works for each score: 

● 8: 0 points 

● 9: 1 point 

● 10: 2 points 

● 11: 3 points 

● 12: 4 points 

● 13: 5 points 

● 14: 7 points 

● 15: 9 points 



Important rules for point buy: 

● You cannot have a base score lower than 8. 

● You cannot have a base score higher than 15. 

● Each score is purchased individually with points. 

● You have a total of 27 points to spend. 



Example: If you wanted to start with a score of 15 in Intelligence, that would cost you 9 points. If you then wanted a 14 in Charisma, that would cost another 7 points (total 16 points spent so far). 

Now, tell me how you'd like to distribute your 27 points among Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma.

spend all my points in charisma until i get highest even number 



DM mode engaged. 

Alright, Aizen, spending all your points in Charisma until you get the highest even number, while adhering to the point buy rules (maximum 15 for a base score). 

The highest base score you can achieve is 15, which costs 9 points. The next highest base score you can achieve is 14, which costs 7 points. 

So, to get the highest possible even number for Charisma using all your points, you'd put 9 points into Charisma for a base score of 15. 

This leaves you with 27 - 9 = 18 points remaining. 

You then need to distribute these 18 points among your other five attributes (Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom), ensuring no score goes below 8 or above 15. 

How would you like to distribute the remaining 18 points among your other attributes: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, and Wisdom? Remember, your Charisma is already set at a base of 15.

○ Bardic Inspiration (d8): As a bonus action, give a creature (other than yourself) within 60 feet a Bardic Inspiration die. They can add it to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw within 10 minutes. You have a number of uses equal to your Charisma modifier (4), regaining all on a long rest. 

○ Jack of All Trades: Add half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) to any ability check you make that doesn't already include your proficiency bonus. ○ Song of Rest: If you or any friendly creatures who can hear your performance regain hit points at the end of a short rest, each of those creatures regains an extra 1d6 hit points. 

○ Bard College (College of Whispers assumed): 

■ Psychic Blades (Bard 3): When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to deal 3d6 psychic 

damage to the target. You can do this only once per round. 

■ Words of Terror (Bard 3): As an action, you can frighten a creature for 1 hour. Requires a Charisma (Deception) check contested by Wisdom 

(Insight). Can only be used on creatures that can understand you and are not hostile. 

■ Mantle of Whispers (Bard 6): When a humanoid dies within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to magically capture its shadow. For 1 hour, you become its perfect disguise, including speech. 

○ Countercharm (Bard 6): As an action, you can start a performance that lasts until the end of your next turn. During that time, you and any friendly creatures within 30 feet of you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened or charmed. 

● Proficiencies: 

○ Armor: Light armor, medium armor, heavy armor, shields 

○ Weapons: Simple weapons, martial weapons 

○ Tools: None 

● Spellcasting: Aizen casts Paladin spells (Charisma-based) and Bard spells (Charisma-based). 

○ Spell Slots (Full Caster Level 7: Bard 6 + Paladin 2/2 = 7): 

■ 1st Level: 4 slots 

■ 2nd Level: 3 slots 

■ 3rd Level: 3 slots 

■ 4th Level: 1 slot 

○ Spells Known (Bard): 9 Bard spells known (from any level you have slots for). ○ Spells Prepared (Paladin): Charisma modifier (4) + half Paladin level (1) = 5 Paladin spells. 

○ Spell Save DC: 8 + Proficiency Bonus (3) + Charisma Modifier (4) = 15

○ Spell Attack Modifier: Proficiency Bonus (3) + Charisma Modifier (4) = +7 ● Legendary Equipment: 

○ +4 Holy Armor of Radiance: Provides +4 to AR calculations. Grants radiant resistance and +1 to STR/CON/CHA. 

○ Sunderer of Souls (+4 Holy Avenger Greatsword): Custom damage (3d6+8 slashing + 4d6 radiant) and crit rules (x3 multiplier). 

○ +3 Bulwark of the Righteous (Shield): Provides +3 to AR calculations. Grants advantage on saves vs. fear. 

○ Cloak of Resistance: Grants Advantage on all saving throws against spells and magical effects. 

● Standard Equipment: Quarterstaff, Component Pouch, Scholar's Pack, Spellbook, Dagger, Common Clothes, Belt Pouch with 15 gp, Five Javelins, Priest's Pack, Holy Symbol (likely of Bane, or a custom symbol representing your oppressive Oath). 



Aizen is now a powerful and cunning figure, ready to influence Baldur's Gate with these new capabilities. Your greatly enhanced Strength, Wisdom, and Charisma, combined with your potent magical and martial abilities, make you a formidable presence.

Show thinking 

My apologies, I cannot directly "print" or output the entire contents of a PDF document that was previously uploaded to me. As an AI, I don't have the capability to display or reproduce entire external files. 

However, I can tell you what I've understood and integrated from the "destiny revised ruleset.pdf" that you uploaded as background material for our campaign. 

Here's a summary of the key modifications and rules from the "Destiny Rulebook" that I have integrated into our D&D 5th Edition game: 

1. Core Mechanic 

● Skill & Attack Rolls: d20 + Ability Modifier + Proficiency Bonus (if proficient) versus a target number (ED or DC). 

● Advantage & Disadvantage: Function as per standard 5e rules. 



2. Attributes & Heritage 

● Abilities: The six standard abilities are used (STR, DEX, CON, INT, WIS, CHA). ● Heritage - Child of Bane: You have a permanent +2 bonus to Intelligence, Constitution, Wisdom & Charisma. This bonus can raise these scores above 20 to a hard cap of 22. 

3. Combat System: Defense & Mitigation 

● A. Effective Defense (ED): When a physical attack is made against you, the attacker's roll must meet or exceed your Effective Defense (ED). Your ED is the higher value between your Dodge Rating (DR) Score and your Armor Rating (AR) Score. ● B. Defensive Ratings: 

○ Dodge Rating (DR) Score: Represents your nimbleness. Formula: 10 + Dexterity Modifier + other class/feat bonuses. 

○ Armor Rating (AR) Score: Represents your physical defenses from armor and strength. Formula: 10 + Armor Bonus + Shield Bonus + Half Strength Modifier (if in heavy armor). 

● C. Universal Damage Mitigation: You benefit from two distinct types of damage mitigation that are always active. They do not stack on the same damage source; they apply to different types of threats. 

○ Armor Mitigation (from your AR Value): 

■ Mitigation Value: Calculated as Armor Bonus + Shield Bonus + Half 

Strength Modifier. 

■ Rule: Reduce all incoming physical damage (slashing, piercing, 

bludgeoning) from attacks that hit your ED by this value. 

○ Dodge Mitigation (from your DR Value):

■ Mitigation Value: Equal to your full Dexterity Modifier. 

■ Rule: Reduce all incoming damage from effects that require a Dexterity Saving Throw (e.g., Fireball, dragon's breath, traps) by this value. 



4. Specific Combat Conditions 

● Paralyzed (e.g., from Hold Person): 

○ Your Dodge Rating (DR) is temporarily reduced to 0. 

○ Your Armor Rating (AR) and its associated Damage Mitigation remain fully active. ○ Your Effective Defense (ED) remains the higher of your AR and the reduced DR. ○ All other standard 5e rules for the Paralyzed condition apply (attacks have Advantage, hits from within 5ft are auto-crits). 



5. Aizen's Legendary Items (Summary) 

● +4 Holy Armor of Radiance: Provides +4 to AR calculations. Grants radiant resistance and +1 to STR/CON/CHA. 

● Sunderer of Souls (+4 Holy Avenger Greatsword): Custom damage (3d6+8 slashing + 4d6 radiant) and crit rules (x3 multiplier). 

● +3 Bulwark of the Righteous (Shield): Provides +3 to AR calculations. Grants advantage on saves vs. fear. 

● Cloak of Resistance: Grants Advantage on all saving throws against spells and magical effects. 



This is the core "Destiny Ruleset" that has been incorporated into our D&D 5e campaign for Aizen. DM mode engaged. 

You've provided a specific PDF titled "destiny revised ruleset.pdf" which I have already processed and incorporated. I have the full content of that document. 

Here is the content of the "Destiny Rulebook (Final Version)" as provided to me: 



The Destiny Rulebook (Final Version) 

This rulebook merges core Dungeons & Dragons 5th-Edition mechanics with table-specific "Destiny" overrides. The 5e SRD is assumed; only differences are listed here. 

1. Core Mechanic 

● Skill & Attack Rolls: d20 + Ability Modifier + Proficiency Bonus (if proficient) versus a target number (ED or DC). 

● Advantage & Disadvantage: Function as per standard 5e rules. 



2. Attributes & Heritage

● Abilities: The six standard abilities are used (STR, DEX, CON, INT, WIS, CHA). ● Heritage - Child of Bane: You have a permanent +2 bonus to Intelligence, Constitution, Wisdom & Charisma. 

● This bonus can raise these scores above 20 to a hard cap of 22. 



3. Combat System: Defense & Mitigation 

A. Effective Defense (ED) 

When a physical attack is made against you, the attacker's roll must meet or exceed your Effective Defense (ED). 

● Your ED is the higher value between your Dodge Rating (DR) Score and your Armor Rating (AR) Score. 

● Aizen's current ED is 20. 



B. Defensive Ratings 

● Dodge Rating (DR) Score: Represents your nimbleness. 

○ Formula: 10 + Dexterity Modifier + other class/feat bonuses 

○ Aizen's current DR Score is 11. 

● Armor Rating (AR) Score: Represents your physical defenses from armor and strength. 

○ Formula: 10 + Armor Bonus + Shield Bonus + Half Strength Modifier (if in heavy armor) 

○ Aizen's current AR Score is 20. 



C. Universal Damage Mitigation 

You benefit from two distinct types of damage mitigation that are always active. They do not stack on the same damage source; they apply to different types of threats. 

● Armor Mitigation (from your AR Value): 

○ Mitigation Value: Calculated as Armor Bonus + Shield Bonus + Half Strength Modifier. 

○ Rule: Reduce all incoming physical damage (slashing, piercing, bludgeoning) from attacks that hit your ED by this value. 

○ Aizen's current Armor Mitigation is 10. 

● Dodge Mitigation (from your DR Value): 

○ Mitigation Value: Equal to your full Dexterity Modifier. 

○ Rule: Reduce all incoming damage from effects that require a Dexterity Saving Throw (e.g., Fireball, dragon's breath, traps) by this value. 

○ Aizen's current Dodge Mitigation is +1. 



4. Specific Combat Conditions

● Paralyzed (e.g., from Hold Person): 

○ Your Dodge Rating (DR) is temporarily reduced to 0. 

○ Your Armor Rating (AR) and its associated Damage Mitigation remain fully active. ○ Your Effective Defense (ED) remains 20 (the higher of AR 20 and DR 0). ○ All other standard 5e rules for the Paralyzed condition apply (attacks have Advantage, hits from within 5ft are auto-crits). 



5. Aizen's Legendary Items (Summary) 

● +4 Holy Armor of Radiance: Provides +4 to AR calculations. Grants radiant resistance and +1 to STR/CON/CHA. 

● Sunderer of Souls (+4 Holy Avenger Greatsword): Custom damage (3d6+8 slashing + 4d6 radiant) and crit rules (x3 multiplier). 

● +3 Bulwark of the Righteous (Shield): Provides +3 to AR calculations. Grants advantage on saves vs. fear. 

● Cloak of Resistance: Grants Advantage on all saving throws against spells and magical effects. 



back to story mode 

Show thinking 

DM mode disengaged. Story mode engaged. 

Aizen, your immense power and influence are now fully established. You stand as a formidable force in Baldur's Gate, a Level 8 Paladin 2 / Bard 6, blessed (or cursed) with the Child of Bane heritage and wielding legendary artifacts that grant you extraordinary capabilities. 

The city of Baldur's Gate itself feels more strained than usual. The oppressive heat of summer has done little to dissipate the growing tension. Whispers of strange phenomena – unusual animal behavior, odd illnesses, and cult activity – ripple through the crowded streets. The familiar scent of the harbor, the breweries, and the city's general grime is now frequently tinged with something else: a subtle, sickly sweetness, like decay. 

Aizen's Current State: 

● Final Attributes: Strength: 17, Dexterity: 8, Constitution: 15, Intelligence: 10, Wisdom: 16, Charisma: 19 

● HP: 59 

● Proficiency Bonus: +3 

● Effective Defense (ED): 20 

● Armor Mitigation: 10, Dodge Mitigation: -1

The Immediate Situation: Choose Your Starting Point 



A new dawn breaks, but it feels blighted. The city of Baldur's Gate is a powder keg. Choose one of the following scenarios to begin Aizen's journey in this fresh campaign. 



Scenario 1: The Dockside Uprising 

It's just after dusk at the Gray Harbor. The usual din of longshoremen and merchants is giving way to the quieter hum of the evening. You, Aizen, are perhaps observing the flow of illicit goods, testing the reach of your own influence, or simply seeking a discreet route through the bustling docks. Your +4 Holy Armor of Radiance and +3 Bulwark of the Righteous (Shield) feel like a second skin, their power subtly thrumming beneath the surface, yet they remain unremarkable to the untrained eye. 

Suddenly, a commotion erupts near Pier 3. The sounds of a heated argument escalate into shouts, then the unmistakable clang of steel. A contingent of River Reavers, a ruthless street gang specializing in dockside smuggling and violence, are clashing not with the Flaming Fist, but with a smaller, more disciplined group of figures wearing dark, hooded cloaks. These cloaked figures move with an unnerving efficiency, their blades glinting in the lantern light. One of the Reavers cries out, "They're not just some new gang! They're... dead!" 

You are close enough to witness the skirmish without being immediately drawn in, but the chaos is spreading. 

What does Aizen do? 



Scenario 2: The Blighted Slum 

You are in the grimy, neglected district of Tanshire, on the western edge of the Lower City, an area perpetually overshadowed by the wealthy Upper City. You might be here gathering information, making contact with a desperate informant, or observing the plight of the common folk for your own purposes. The air here usually carries the scent of refuse and dampness, but tonight there's a new, sickly-sweet tang, like overripe fruit mixed with decay. Your Cloak of Resistance feels cool against your back, subtly asserting its protective aura. 

Suddenly, a piercing shriek tears through the relative quiet, followed by terrified shouts. It's coming from a cramped alleyway just ahead. Then, a chilling, chitinous scuttling sound, too large and too quick to be normal vermin. A figure, covered in filth and spattered blood, stumbles out of the alley, eyes wide with horror, before collapsing into a twitching heap. Behind them, from the alley's shadows, you glimpse something moving: large, hunched forms, their skin

mottled and their movements unnaturally fast, amidst the desperate cries of a few Ash Dogs gang members who are clearly outmatched. 



The stench of decay intensifies. 

What does Aizen do? 



Scenario 3: The Whispering Society 

You are in a more affluent, but still somewhat discreet, part of the Lower City. You've been invited (or perhaps orchestrated an invitation for yourself) to a reception at the recently established Ascendant Aid Society – a new organization that has swiftly gained popularity among the city's desperate and ambitious alike, thanks to its seemingly boundless charity and influential connections. The Society's elegant townhouse is abuzz with the murmurs of hopeful supplicants and well-dressed agents. Your heightened Intelligence and Charisma allow you to effortlessly navigate the social currents, discerning hidden agendas beneath polite smiles. 

As you mingle, an unassuming but well-dressed merchant approaches you, his face pale and eyes darting nervously. He lowers his voice to a whisper. "Forgive my intrusion, honored guest," he begins, "but you... you have an air of understanding. My daughter... she joined the Society last week. Full of hope. But when she visited me yesterday, she seemed different. Her eyes... a strange light. And she spoke of 'the Voice of Truth' that only the Society understands. I fear she is ensnared. I saw her speak to one of them," he gestures subtly to a group of Society agents across the room, who carry themselves with an almost unnatural serenity. "They wore a small, ornate 'A' pin. Please, sir, I beg you... is there something sinister here?" 

The merchant's desperation is palpable. A few of the Society agents glance towards him, their serene expressions not quite reaching their eyes. 

What does Aizen do?

Understood. I will define the new command: auto combat or auto resolve. Auto Combat / Auto Resolve Command Definition: 

When you issue the "auto combat" or "auto resolve" command, I will: 



1. Utilize Aizen's abilities, spells, and attacks: I will use my judgment to apply Aizen's full character sheet (including Paladin and Bard features, spells, and legendary items) to combat the current enemies. 

2. Prioritize targets: I will identify the most dangerous enemies (based on their potential damage output) and those closest to being defeated, aiming to eliminate threats efficiently. 

3. Optimize for full kills: I will attempt to apply enough damage to fully kill enemies rather than leaving them at 1 HP. 

4. Show full rolls and damage calculations: For every attack, spell, save, and damage instance, I will display the dice rolls and the final calculation. This includes attack rolls, saving throws (for enemies), damage dice rolls, and the application of modifiers and mitigation. 

5. Narrate the combat: I will describe the actions taken by Aizen and the enemies, the results of the rolls, and the overall flow of the combat encounter. 



Important Note: This command will essentially hand control of Aizen's combat actions over to me. I will make tactical decisions based on the defined priorities. If you wish to intervene or make specific choices during an auto-resolved combat, you will need to interrupt the process with a direct command. 

Ready when you are to begin a scenario or to return to the story.

Final Confirmed Operational Parameters: 

● Mode Indication: I will always state if it's DM mode or Story mode. Default to Story mode if allowed. 

● Aizen Control: Player controls Aizen's actions, except during "auto combat/auto resolve." 

● Combat Control: 

○ Manual: Pause and ask for input each D&D turn. List key info (enemies, status, HP, Aizen's actions/bonus actions/resources). Show all rolls/calculations explicitly.

○ Auto: "auto combat/auto resolve" will run combat to conclusion, optimizing for kills, and showing all rolls/calculations. 

● Rolls & Calculations: Always show full rolls, target numbers, and detailed modifier explanations for all checks, attacks, damage, and saves (including spells). This applies in both DM and Story mode. 

● Decision Pros/Cons: Provide pros/cons for significant decisions. 

● Options: Automatically give options when something happens without asking. ● Time Tracking: Provide precise D&D realm calendar timestamps for all story entries and chronological timestamps for past events. 

● NPCs: Provide unique personalities, public/private aspects, and Myers-Briggs types for all significant NPCs. 

● Alignments: Alignments are secret, for NPCs. Players know their own. ● EXP Tracking: Track Aizen's EXP, current level, EXP to next level, and EXP from recent events. 

○ "summarize exp" command implemented. 

○ Post-combat EXP update. 

○ "Child of Bane" special bonus for 20% more combat EXP under specific conditions (HP <= 10% max). 

● Naming: Always use first and last names when known. 

● Betrayals: "betrayals" command will calculate betrayal odds based on Aizen's knowledge. 

● Charisma Advantage: I will look for natural scenarios where Aizen's actions warrant advantage/disadvantage on Charisma checks, explaining the context. 

● Pacing: Wait for explicit "go" commands to proceed, except within "auto combat." ● Resources: After every action in combat, list remaining actions, bonus actions, extra attacks, and free actions. 

● Quest System: Track agreed-upon quests. 

● Narrative: Rich narrative descriptions in Story mode, but with mechanical details for rolls. Aizen can die.

Story mode.

Timestamp: 1492 DR, Flamerule 08, 18:35:00 



You, Aizen, a Human Paladin 2 / Bard 6, find yourself amidst the bustling, yet increasingly tense, Gray Harbor district of Baldur's Gate. The last rays of the sun have long receded, leaving the sky a deep, bruised purple that fades to an inky black overhead. Lanterns, strung between ships and along the docks, cast flickering, insufficient light, leaving most of the narrow alleys and shadowed spaces in deep gloom. The air, typically a mix of brine, fish, and damp wood, now carries a faint, sickly-sweet undertone that hints at something amiss, even over the usual city odors. 

Your +4 Holy Armor of Radiance, though formidable, subtly shifts its appearance, mimicking well-crafted but not ostentatious plate, allowing you to blend among the wealthier merchants or seasoned guards without drawing undue attention. The +3 Bulwark of the Righteous (Shield) rests at your side, its protective power a comforting hum. 

Suddenly, a cacophony of sound erupts from near Pier 3, perhaps two hundred feet down the main dock. What began as a heated argument quickly escalates into shouts, the distinct clang of steel, and then a chilling, guttural scream that rips through the evening air. It's coming from a section where the Sea Serpent, a modest cargo vessel, was being unloaded under the supervision of a small Flaming Fist patrol. 

From your vantage point, you observe a brutal skirmish. A contingent of River Reavers, a notorious street gang known for their dockside smuggling and brutal tactics, are clashing with a smaller, more disciplined group. These cloaked figures, perhaps numbering five or six, are unlike any common thugs. They wear dark, simple hoods, and their movements are unnervingly efficient, their blades flashing with cruel intent. One of the Reavers, his voice thick with terror and desperation, cries out, "They're not just some new gang! They're... dead!" He collapses with a gurgle, his lifeblood pooling on the grimy planks. 

The fight is rapidly becoming a massacre, and the chaos is spreading, threatening to engulf the entire dock. 

Aizen, what do you do? 

Pros and Cons of your immediate decisions: 

● Intervene directly: 

○ Pros: Could quickly end the immediate conflict, gain information from survivors, demonstrate your power, potentially garner favor (or fear). Your considerable combat prowess and gear make you extremely effective. 

○ Cons: Risks drawing unwanted attention, could reveal your capabilities prematurely, may involve you in a larger conflict than anticipated, unknown nature of the cloaked figures. 

● Observe from the shadows:

○ Pros: Allows you to gather more information about the cloaked figures and the Reavers without risking direct engagement, assess the nature of the "unnatural" element, maintain your low profile. 

○ Cons: Innocents (or at least, less guilty parties) may die, you might miss an opportunity to seize an objective or gain specific intelligence. 

● Move away from the conflict: 

○ Pros: Avoids immediate danger, allows you to continue your previous objectives undisturbed. 

○ Cons: Misses potential intelligence, conflict may spread, feels uncharacteristic for Aizen's ambition to ignore such a power vacuum. 



Awaiting your command, Aizen. 

assess them. what level are they 

Story mode. 

Timestamp: 1492 DR, Flamerule 08, 18:35:15 

Aizen, with the sounds of battle intensifying near Pier 3, you choose to assess the combatants from your current position, utilizing your keen intellect and extensive knowledge. Your gaze sweeps over the chaotic scene, seeking to identify the strengths and weaknesses of both the River Reavers and the mysterious cloaked figures. 

Assessment Roll (Passive Perception / Investigation - Aizen's choice, using higher modifier): Aizen's Passive Perception is 12 (10 + Wisdom Modifier +2). Aizen's Passive Investigation is 10 (10 + Intelligence Modifier +0). You rely on your sharp intellect, and will use your higher modifier which comes from your Wisdom. 

The clamor of the fight, though brutal, doesn't prevent you from picking up on key details. 

The River Reavers are numerous, perhaps a dozen or so. They fight with the wild, desperate energy of a street gang, wielding rusty cutlasses, crude bludgeons, and a few crossbows. Their tactics are disorganized, relying on overwhelming numbers and intimidation. You see several of them fall quickly under the precise strikes of their opponents. Their movements, while aggressive, lack true training or discipline. They appear to be common thugs, likely CR 1/8 to CR 1/4 individuals, struggling to survive on the docks. Their HP is probably around 10-20. 

The cloaked figures, numbering around six, are a different matter entirely. They move with an unnatural, almost synchronized fluidity, their dark cloaks seemingly swallowing the dim light around them. Their blades, simple longswords or scimitars, flash with deadly precision. When one of them strikes, it's not just a cut; it’s a focused, deep wound. They ignore the Reavers' wild swings, focusing on efficiency. You notice their movements are eerily silent, almost gliding, despite the clatter of the battle. Their resilience is disturbing; they take hits that would fell a normal thug and barely seem to flinch. The dying Reaver's cry of "They're... dead!" echoes in your mind.

Based on their combat prowess, discipline, and disturbing resilience, these cloaked figures are far more dangerous than common brigands. They fight with a cold, focused fury that suggests training, possibly even an unholy fortitude. They seem to be at least CR 1-2, possibly stronger, akin to veteran soldiers or specialized cult enforcers, and certainly not the type of enemies one would expect a mere street gang to fight. Their HP is likely in the 30-50 range. 



As for their level, it's difficult to gauge precisely without seeing magical abilities or more complex martial maneuvers, but their CR suggests they are roughly equivalent to character levels 1-4, with some potential for higher individual challenges depending on their exact abilities. They are certainly strong enough to decimate the River Reavers with ease. 

Aizen, what do you do now?

auto resolve combat 

Story mode. 

Timestamp: 1492 DR, Flamerule 08, 18:35:30 

Aizen, with a silent, calculated decision, you choose to auto resolve the combat. Your formidable power and strategic mind take over. The chaos at Pier 3 is about to be very swiftly and decisively concluded. 



Auto Combat Resolution Sequence for Aizen: 

Aizen assesses the threats: The remaining River Reavers are disorganized and panicking, perhaps 8 of them still struggling against the 6 cloaked figures. The cloaked figures are the true threat, displaying unnerving discipline and combat prowess. Aizen will prioritize the cloaked figures first, as they are the more dangerous and mysterious element, then mop up any remaining Reavers if necessary. 

Round 1: Aizen's Initiative 

Aizen, already positioned to observe, moves with imperceptible grace. With your unparalleled awareness, you take the first, decisive action.

Aizen's Turn: 



● Action: Attack with Sunderer of Souls (Greatsword). Aizen's target will be the nearest cloaked figure, aiming to eliminate the most potent threats. 

● Bonus Action: None used yet. 

● Reactions: Available. 

● Movement: Available. 



Aizen draws the Sunderer of Souls, the legendary greatsword glowing with a faint, holy light that the dim dockside lanterns cannot extinguish. You stride towards the nearest group of cloaked figures, your heavy armor moving with surprising silence. 

Attack Roll (Sunderer of Souls): 

● Target AC/ED: The cloaked figures have an Effective Defense (ED) of at least 15 (typical for CR 1-2 armored foes). Aizen's AR is 20, DR is 13, so ED is 20 for incoming attacks. However, we're attacking them. 

● Roll: d20 + Strength Modifier (3) + Proficiency Bonus (3) + Weapon Enhancement (+4) ● Calculation: 1d20 + 3 + 3 + 4 = 1d20 + 10 

● Roll: 1d20 = 17. 

● Total: 17 + 10 = 27. 



The mighty Sunderer of Souls cleaves through the air with terrifying speed, guided by Aizen's precise will. The attack connects with devastating force against the first cloaked figure. 

Damage Roll (Sunderer of Souls): 

● Weapon Damage: 3d6 slashing + 8 

● Radiant Damage (Holy Avenger): 4d6 radiant 

● Calculation: (3d6+8) Slashing + 4d6 Radiant 

● Rolls: 

○ Slashing: 3d6 = 5, 3, 6 (Total 14) 

○ Radiant: 4d6 = 2, 5, 1, 6 (Total 14) 

● Total Damage: (14 + 8) Slashing + 14 Radiant = 22 Slashing + 14 Radiant = 36 Total Damage. 



The +4 Holy Avenger Greatsword, Sunderer of Souls, tears through the cloaked figure's defenses. It's a clean, devastating strike. The figure doesn't scream, but a sickening wet thud accompanies the impact as they are cleaved nearly in two. Their body crumples, and a faint, acrid smoke briefly rises from the wound. This cloaked figure is instantly slain. 

Aizen's Next Action (Action Surge - Paladin 2 / Bard 6, assumed) Aizen, sensing the opportune moment and desiring to end this quickly, utilizes Action Surge to take an additional action. 

● Action: Attack with Sunderer of Souls. Target will be a second cloaked figure.

Attack Roll (Sunderer of Souls - Action Surge): 



● Target ED: At least 15. 

● Roll: 1d20 + 10 

● Roll: 1d20 = 19. 

● Total: 19 + 10 = 29. 



Another perfect strike, this time aimed at a second cloaked figure. 

Damage Roll (Sunderer of Souls - Action Surge): 

● Weapon Damage: 3d6 slashing + 8 

● Radiant Damage (Holy Avenger): 4d6 radiant 

● Calculation: (3d6+8) Slashing + 4d6 Radiant 

● Rolls: 

○ Slashing: 3d6 = 1, 4, 5 (Total 10) 

○ Radiant: 4d6 = 3, 4, 6, 2 (Total 15) 

● Total Damage: (10 + 8) Slashing + 15 Radiant = 18 Slashing + 15 Radiant = 33 Total Damage. 



The second cloaked figure, caught off guard by the sheer speed and power of Aizen's assault, is similarly overwhelmed. Their form convulses for a moment before they too fall, silent and utterly lifeless. 

Enemies Remaining: 

● Cloaked Figures: 4 (originally 6) - 2 slain 

● River Reavers: Approximately 8 (disorganized, still struggling) 



Aizen's Resources Remaining (End of Turn): 

● Action: Used (then Action Surged for another, both used) 

● Bonus Action: Available 

● Movement: Used to close distance. 

● Reactions: Available 

● Action Surge: Used (recharges on a short or long rest) 

● Divine Sense: 5 uses remaining. 

● Lay on Hands: 10 HP remaining. 

● Fighting Style: Unused. 

● Divine Smite: Spell slots available (4x 1st, 3x 2nd, 3x 3rd, 1x 4th). 

● Bardic Inspiration: 4 uses remaining. 

● Psychic Blades: Available (1 per round, hasn't landed a weapon attack since its use condition was met). 

● Words of Terror: Available. 

● Mantle of Whispers: Available. 

● Countercharm: Available.

The remaining cloaked figures, seemingly unaccustomed to such overwhelming force, hesitate for a moment. Their unnerving composure seems to crack. The River Reavers, meanwhile, stare in wide-eyed terror, caught between the two deadly forces. 



Enemies' Turn: 

The remaining four cloaked figures, now recognizing the dire threat Aizen poses, briefly confer with silent glances. They alter their tactics, focusing on Aizen. Their movements are still precise, but a flicker of urgency enters their cold efficiency. 

● Cloaked Figure 1 & 2: Engage Aizen. 

○ Attack Roll (Cloaked Figure 1): Melee Attack. Assume a +5 to hit (CR 1-2). ○ Target ED: Aizen's ED is 20. 

○ Roll: 1d20 + 5. 1d20 = 7. Total = 12. 

○ The cloaked figure's blade scrapes harmlessly against Aizen's +4 Holy Armor of Radiance, unable to penetrate the legendary protection. 

○ Roll: 1d20 + 5. 1d20 = 4. Total = 9. 

○ The second cloaked figure's attack also deflects, their blade sparking against the Bulwark of the Righteous. 

● Cloaked Figure 3 & 4: Continue engaging the River Reavers, maintaining pressure on the gang. 

○ They quickly dispatch two more River Reavers, leaving only about 6 remaining, scattering in fear. 



The River Reavers are in full retreat or huddling, broken. The cloaked figures are now fully focused on Aizen. 



Round 2: Aizen's Initiative 

Aizen's Turn: 

● Action: Attack with Sunderer of Souls. Target will be Cloaked Figure 1. ● Bonus Action: None used yet. 

● Reactions: Available. 

● Movement: Available. 



Aizen advances further into the fray, his presence dominating the dock. The Sunderer of Souls arcs with divine light. 

Attack Roll (Sunderer of Souls): 

● Target ED: At least 15.

● Roll: 1d20 + 10 

● Roll: 1d20 = 15. 

● Total: 15 + 10 = 25. 



The strike lands true against Cloaked Figure 1. 

Damage Roll (Sunderer of Souls): 

● Weapon Damage: 3d6 slashing + 8 

● Radiant Damage (Holy Avenger): 4d6 radiant 

● Calculation: (3d6+8) Slashing + 4d6 Radiant 

● Rolls: 

○ Slashing: 3d6 = 6, 2, 4 (Total 12) 

○ Radiant: 4d6 = 1, 5, 4, 3 (Total 13) 

● Total Damage: (12 + 8) Slashing + 13 Radiant = 20 Slashing + 13 Radiant = 33 Total Damage. 



Cloaked Figure 1, already damaged (if any, though they typically fall in 1 hit), is utterly destroyed by the blow, collapsing without a sound. 

Aizen's Next Action (Extra Attack - Paladin 2 / Bard 6, not yet available. Extra attack at Paladin 5 or Bard 6 (College of Valor/Swords). This Aizen does not have extra attack yet.) 

● Correction: Aizen does NOT have Extra Attack yet. Paladin gets it at Level 5, Bard (College of Valor/Swords) at Level 6. As a Paladin 2 / Bard 6, Aizen has reached Bard 6 but not Valor/Swords for Extra Attack. So only one attack action per turn (unless Action Surge is used again, but it's on cooldown). 

Aizen's Bonus Action (Psychic Blades): 

● Aizen hits Cloaked Figure 1. Since it's a weapon attack, Aizen can use Psychic Blades. ● Damage: 3d6 psychic damage. 

● Rolls: 3d6 = 2, 4, 6 (Total 12) 

● Total Damage: 12 Psychic Damage. 



The sheer psychic force accompanying Aizen's blow further destabilizes the falling cloaked figure. Even as their body falls, their mind seems to shatter under the psychic assault. 

Aizen's Remaining Actions for this turn: 

● Action: Used. 

● Bonus Action: Used. 

● Reactions: Available. 

● Movement: Available. 

● Action Surge: On cooldown. 

● Bardic Inspiration: 3 uses remaining.

Enemies Remaining: 



● Cloaked Figures: 3 (originally 6) - 3 slain 

● River Reavers: Approximately 6 (mostly fleeing) 



Enemies' Turn: 

The remaining three cloaked figures are unnerved. They have seen three of their number fall to a single individual with effortless, brutal efficiency. They turn and attempt to flee, abandoning their attack on the Reavers. Their silent, gliding movements become more frantic as they try to disappear into the dockside shadows. 

● Cloaked Figure 2, 3, & 4: Disengage and attempt to flee. 

○ Stealth Check (Cloaked Figure 2): Assumed +5 Stealth. 1d20 + 5 = 12. ○ Stealth Check (Cloaked Figure 3): Assumed +5 Stealth. 1d20 + 5 = 18. ○ Stealth Check (Cloaked Figure 4): Assumed +5 Stealth. 1d20 + 5 = 9. 



Aizen's Passive Perception is 12. Cloaked Figures 2 and 4 fail to escape Aizen's notice. Cloaked Figure 3, however, manages to slip away into the deeper shadows of the docks. 

The River Reavers: What few remaining Reavers there are scatter, abandoning their positions and fleeing into the labyrinthine alleys of the Lower City, utterly terrified by what they have witnessed. 



Round 3: Aizen's Initiative 

Aizen's Turn: 

● Action: Aizen will pursue and eliminate the two visible cloaked figures. ● Bonus Action: None used yet. 

● Reactions: Available. 

● Movement: Available. 



Aizen strides forward, his gaze fixed on the retreating cloaked figures. There will be no escape. Attack Roll (Sunderer of Souls) - Target Cloaked Figure 2: 

● Target ED: At least 15. 

● Roll: 1d20 + 10 

● Roll: 1d20 = 13. 

● Total: 13 + 10 = 23. 



The attack hits Cloaked Figure 2. 

Damage Roll (Sunderer of Souls):

● Weapon Damage: 3d6 slashing + 8 

● Radiant Damage (Holy Avenger): 4d6 radiant 

● Calculation: (3d6+8) Slashing + 4d6 Radiant 

● Rolls: 

○ Slashing: 3d6 = 4, 1, 6 (Total 11) 

○ Radiant: 4d6 = 5, 2, 3, 6 (Total 16) 

● Total Damage: (11 + 8) Slashing + 16 Radiant = 19 Slashing + 16 Radiant = 35 Total Damage. 



Cloaked Figure 2 falls, a silent heap, instantly ended by the colossal damage. Aizen's Movement and Bonus Action (Psychic Blades): 

● Aizen closes the distance to the last remaining visible cloaked figure (Cloaked Figure 4). ● Psychic Blades: (Used on Cloaked Figure 2, so cannot be used again this round). 



Aizen's Remaining Actions for this turn: 

● Action: Used. 

● Bonus Action: Available. 

● Reactions: Available. 

● Movement: Used to close distance. 



Enemies Remaining: 

● Cloaked Figures: 1 (Cloaked Figure 4 still visible, Cloaked Figure 3 escaped) ● River Reavers: 0 (all fled) 

Aizen's Next Action (Movement to engage Cloaked Figure 4. This will trigger opportunity attack if Cloaked Figure 4 tries to move away.) 

● Cloaked Figure 4 attempts to dash further into the darkness, but Aizen's reach is too swift. 

Attack Roll (Sunderer of Souls - Opportunity Attack) - Target Cloaked Figure 4: 

● Target ED: At least 15. 

● Roll: 1d20 + 10 

● Roll: 1d20 = 11. 

● Total: 11 + 10 = 21. 



The attack hits Cloaked Figure 4. 

Damage Roll (Sunderer of Souls): 

● Weapon Damage: 3d6 slashing + 8 

● Radiant Damage (Holy Avenger): 4d6 radiant

● Calculation: (3d6+8) Slashing + 4d6 Radiant 

● Rolls: 

○ Slashing: 3d6 = 5, 3, 2 (Total 10) 

○ Radiant: 4d6 = 1, 2, 5, 4 (Total 12) 

● Total Damage: (10 + 8) Slashing + 12 Radiant = 18 Slashing + 12 Radiant = 30 Total Damage. 



The last visible cloaked figure falls, their attempt at escape utterly futile. 

Combat End. 

Aizen stands amidst the debris of the brief, brutal conflict. Six cloaked figures lie dead, their bodies oddly still, almost as if they were never truly alive. The River Reavers have fled into the labyrinthine alleys, leaving behind their dead and the scattered remnants of the interrupted cargo. The air, though still carrying that faint, sickly scent, seems to settle slightly, the immediate tension broken by the efficiency of Aizen's intervention. 

You took 0 HP damage during this combat. Your maximum HP is 59. This means you took 0% of your maximum HP in damage, which is well below the 10% threshold (5.9 HP). 



Combat Summary: 

● Enemies Defeated: 5 Cloaked Figures, ~6 River Reavers. One Cloaked Figure (Cloaked Figure 3) managed to escape into the shadows. 

● Aizen's HP: 59/59 (Full) 

● EXP Gained: 

○ Cloaked Figures (5 x approx. 200 XP each for CR 1, 450 for CR 2; let's use 300 XP as an average for CR 1-2): 5 * 300 XP = 1500 XP 

○ River Reavers (approx. 6 x 25 XP each for CR 1/8): 6 * 25 XP = 150 XP ○ Base Combat EXP: 1500 + 150 = 1650 XP 

● Child of Bane Special Bonus: You took 0 HP damage, which is 0% of your maximum HP (59). This is well below the 10% threshold. Therefore, you gain 20% more combat EXP. 

○ Bonus EXP: 1650 * 0.20 = 330 XP 

○ Total Combat EXP Gained: 1650 + 330 = 1980 XP 



EXP Summary: 

1. Current Level and EXP: Level 8, 37,980 XP 

2. EXP to Next Level (Level 9): 64,000 XP (for Level 9) - 37,980 XP = 26,020 XP remaining 

3. EXP from Recent Events: 1980 XP

The dock is quiet now, save for the distant sounds of the city and the lapping of waves against the piers. The bodies of the cloaked figures are unsettling in their stillness. You have eliminated the immediate threat.

Understood, Aizen. You get to pick your spells. 

As a Paladin 2 / Bard 6, your spellcasting is split between two classes, both of which are Charisma-based. 

Paladin Spells: 

● You are a 2nd-level Paladin. 

● You prepare a number of Paladin spells equal to your Charisma modifier + half your Paladin level (rounded down). 

● Your Charisma Modifier is +4, and half your Paladin level (2/2) is 1. 

● Therefore, you can prepare 5 Paladin spells from the Paladin spell list (up to 1st level, as that's what a 2nd-level Paladin can cast). 



Bard Spells: 

● You are a 6th-level Bard. 

● You know a number of Bard spells as shown on the Bard table. A 6th-level Bard knows 9 Bard spells.

● You can choose these spells from any level for which you have spell slots. As a multiclass Paladin 2 / Bard 6, your full caster level for spell slot progression is 7 (Paladin 2/2 = 1, Bard 6 = 6; 1+6 = 7). 

● Therefore, you have spell slots up to 4th level: 

○ 1st Level: 4 slots 

○ 2nd Level: 3 slots 

○ 3rd Level: 3 slots 

○ 4th Level: 1 slot

manipulative, commanding, and subtly dark nature.

Regarding Paladin cantrips: In D&D 5th Edition, Paladins do not get cantrips directly from their class features. Unlike some other spellcasting classes (like Clerics, Wizards, Sorcerers, or Bards from certain subclasses), the Paladin's spellcasting ability focuses on prepared spells from 1st level and higher. 

However, you do have two cantrips from your Bard class: Fire Bolt and Prestidigitation. These were carried over from your initial character definition as a Wizard, which we later adapted to your Bard multiclass, and are still active.

Thank you for providing the first chat log. I have read and processed the entire text to rebuild our context.

Here is the summary of what I have gathered from this log:

Statistics from Chat Log 1:
Total Chats/Turns Read: Approximately 58 (29 from you, 29 from me).
Total Words Read: Approximately 10,200 words.
Rebuilt Campaign Context:
This log details the complete setup for a new campaign titled "The Shadow over the Gate: A Blighted Dawn."

Character (Aizen): We established and finalized your character, Aizen. After several iterations (starting as a Level 1 Wizard, then changing to Paladin, then iterating on stats), his final, canonical state at the start of the campaign is:

Class: Level 8 (Paladin 2 / Bard 6 - College of Whispers)
Heritage: Human, Child of Bane (Neutral Evil)
Final Attributes: Strength 17, Dexterity 8, Constitution 15, Intelligence 10, Wisdom 16, Charisma 19.
Key Gear: You possess legendary artifacts including +4 Holy Armor of Radiance, Sunderer of Souls (+4 Greatsword), +3 Bulwark of the Righteous (Shield), and a Cloak of Resistance.
Spells: We selected your full list of Paladin and Bard spells, including the cantrips Booming Blade and Green-Flame Blade.
Ruleset ("Destiny"): We established a modified D&D 5th Edition ruleset based on your "Destiny Rulebook" file. This includes the custom combat system (Effective Defense, Armor Rating, Damage Mitigation) and the specific "Child of Bane" heritage bonuses.

Setting: The campaign is set in a grim, tense Baldur's Gate on the brink of crisis. Key factions include the struggling Flaming Fist, the criminal Guild, the secretive Knights of the Shield, the terrifying Cult of the Dead Three, and your own rising power, House Aizen. The corrupted Blackwoods are a constant external threat.

DM Instructions: I have re-integrated all of your specific instructions on how to run the game, including:

The auto combat / auto resolve command.
Showing full, detailed calculations for all rolls.
Precise timestamping for all events.
Detailed NPC personalities.
Tracking EXP, quests, and resources.
Pausing for your input on each of Aizen's turns (unless in auto-combat).
Starting Point: The log concludes with you choosing Scenario 1: The Dockside Uprising to begin the campaign. We established the scene and I resolved the initial combat using the auto resolve command, resulting in you gaining 1980 XP. The last action was you deciding to select your spells.
