Some responses below were generated according to the creator's saved info and preferences. Learn more Opens in a new window June 9, 2025 at 05:09 PM Published June 12, 2025 at 11:37 PM



I was using gemini chat to play a modified D&D campaign with special rules. I want to continue playing the campaign in this chat window and provide the context/files to continue seamlessly so I don’t even realized I made a new chat. I will provide

Overall campaign information

Full chat log of the previous chat

Reference files I upload. If there are any conflicts/confusion with them please let me know!

I am uploading books from game of thrones or song of ice and fire series. They are for style and do not use actual characters, places etc from them.

The main prompts/instructions I used in the campaign I will send again.

Excellent. I am ready to continue your Dungeons & Dragons campaign. Please provide all the necessary information, and I will do my best to ensure a seamless and immersive experience, picking up right where you left off.

Please send over the following at your convenience:

Overall Campaign Information: A summary of the world, the main plot, key NPCs, and any house rules or modifications to the D&D system you are using.

Full Chat Log: The complete history of your previous chat session. This is essential for me to understand the narrative so far, your character's actions, and the established tone.

Reference Files: I will review any documents you upload. I understand the "A Song of Ice and Fire" books are for stylistic inspiration and I will not incorporate specific characters or locations from them. Should any conflicts or points of confusion arise from these files, I will be sure to ask for clarification.

Main Prompts/Instructions: The specific prompts you were using to guide the campaign. This will ensure I adhere to the established rules and your preferred playstyle.

Once I have reviewed all the materials, I will let you know if I have any questions. Otherwise, we can dive straight back into your adventure. I look forward to continuing the story with you.

Here is the complete export of your campaign, detailing all relevant information, established rules, character profiles, systems, and the current state of your dominion.

Campaign Export: Aizen - The God-King of Absolute Codification (Definitive)

I. Campaign Core Premise

You are Aizen, the newly ascended God-King of Absolute Codification, Tyranny, and Control. You achieved this by successfully consuming the essence of Bane (Divine Entity). Your ultimate goal is to consolidate your dominion over the cosmos, impose your absolute order, and ascend to Transcendent Greater Deity status within one year.

II. Aizen: The God-King Character Profile

You operate primarily in a mortal disguise but possess your full divine power.

Divine Rank: Nascent Greater Deity

Ascension Trigger: Character Level 12 (Paladin 2 / Bard 10).

Current Mortal/Class Level (for context): Level 12 (Paladin 2 / Bard 10), 113,440 XP.

Proficiency Bonus (PB): +6 (Transcendental, based on Level 20 mortal baseline, reflecting mastery beyond mortal limits).

Divine Charisma Equivalent: Your consumed divine essence imbues you with an inherent charisma equivalent to 30 (Modifier: +10).

Effective Divine Charisma (for Elminster/Mystra interaction, current): 35 (Modifier: +12) - This is a temporary bonus from preparation for this specific interaction.

Divine Resilience (DR): 750 / 750 (Full)

Divine Armor Class (DAC): 25

Divine Power Points (DPP): 715 DPP remaining today (Daily replenishment is 825 DPP).

Current Mortal HP: 59/59 (Full). AC 18.

Spell Slots: 1st: 4, 2nd: 3, 3rd: 3, 4th: 3, 5th: 1, 6th: 1 (all full).

Bardic Inspiration: 6 uses remaining.

Divine Sense: 7 uses remaining.

Lay on Hands: 10 HP remaining.

Great Weapon Master: Ready. Extra Attack: Active.

Kyōka Suigetsu: Active.

Key Divine Abilities & Features (All Active):

Divine Save DC (for influencing others): 100 (Absolute & Unbeatable for all mortals).

Divine Imposition (Consumed Will): When influencing mortals, you do not roll. The target makes a Divine Saving Throw (Wisdom or Charisma, whichever is lower for them) against your DC 100 with Disadvantage. If they somehow succeed, you can force a reroll (taking the lower result) via Divine Intervention: Consumed Will (once per turn).

Divine Awareness: Passive awareness of major cosmic events, significant shifts in divine power, and the presence of powerful divine beings within a vast, multi-planar radius.

Divine Insight into Cosmic Order: Unparalleled understanding of cosmic laws and inter-deity relations.

Divine Ability Checks: For any ability check related to the mortal realm (Investigation, Perception, Arcana, History, Insight, Survival), your divine intellect automatically succeeds against any DC (treated as DC 100). No dice rolls are made.

Domain Influence (Conceptual): Your influence extends to the very concepts of Tyranny, Control, and Order through Absolute Will, and Absolute Codification. You can inherently bend these concepts to your will.

Child of Bane (Heritage Special Bonus): Permanent +2 bonus to Intelligence, Constitution, Wisdom & Charisma (can raise scores above 20 to 22). 20% more combat EXP.

Personality (As Codified by You - from your uploaded file):

Myers-Briggs Type (Archetype): INTJ-ENTP Hinge (The Architect / The Debater).

Core Nature: A philosopher-king, master of systems, views the world as a flawed schematic to be perfected. Ruthless, analytical, driven by the imposition of his will.

Masks:The Cultured Scholar & Courteous Advisor: Default public interface, disarming gentility, flawless etiquette, uses subtle manipulation.

The Benevolent Tyrant: To those he controls, he offers order and purpose, suppressing chaos with an iron, yet seemingly calm, fist.

True Self: Unquenchable ambition, intellectual superiority, chilling detachment, profound enjoyment in breaking wills and imposing order.

Motivations: Ascend to ultimate power, establish absolute order, dismantle and reassemble existence, confirm his own superiority.

Fears: Stagnation, defiance to his will, the unforeseen breaking of his own systems.

III. Quantified Divine Attributes & Combat Mechanics

A. Divine Ranks & Attributes:

Divine RankDivine Resilience (DR)Divine Armor Class (DAC)Divine Power Points (DPP/day)Divine Attack/Influence Roll (DAIR Mod)Divine Legendary Resistances (per day)Primary Divine Damage (DR)Example Deities (Typical)Lesser Deity1502050+15 (1d20 + Div Mod + 10 + PB)110 + (1d20 * 2)Garl Glittergold, Lliira, TymoraIntermediate Deity30022200+20 (1d20 + Div Mod + 14 + PB)320 + (1d20 * 3)Torm, Lathander, Myrkul, Helm, CorellonNascent Greater Deity (YOU)75025825+31 (1d20 + 10 (Div CHA) + 15 (Rank) + 6 (PB))480 + (1d20 * 5)Aizen, The God-KingGreater Deity1000+25+1000++25+ (1d20 + Div Mod + 19 + PB)5+30 + (1d20 * 5)+Mystra, Shar, Tempus, Tiamat, OghmaAo (The Overgod)InfiniteInfiniteInfiniteInfiniteInfiniteInfiniteN/A (Beyond Pantheon)

Export to Sheets

B. God Combat Mechanics:

Divine Attack/Influence Roll (DAIR): Your primary roll for direct, damaging, or major influential actions against other divine beings.

Divine Damage: When your DAIR hits another deity.

Divine Action Economy: Gods can take 1 major Divine Action per round (costing DPP). They can also take 3 Divine Legendary Actions per round (minor actions, 10-25 DPP each).

Major Divine Action (Cost):Your Cost (Aizen): 100-150 DPP.

Lesser Deity: 25-50 DPP.

Intermediate Deity: 50-100 DPP.

Greater Deity: 150-250 DPP.

IV. Campaign Rules & DM Notes

System: Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition (adapted).

Destiny Rulebook: Merges D&D 5e with table-specific "Destiny" overrides (ED, DR, AR, Mitigation). Original destiny revised ruleset.pdf.

DM Warnings (Proactive):You will be warned 4 hours before the start of a new day (when your DPP refreshes).

You will be warned if something in the narrative references or uses your original Level 12 mortal character's mechanics, stats, or specific context.

You will be warned if your considered alliances could invoke the wrath of powerful gods (adhering to "friendly terms, avoid entangling alliances" principle).

Divine Perception of Gods: You do not perceive the "true intentions" or hidden sides of other gods. You deduce their likely objectives and dispositions from their domain, actions, and expressed will.

Timestamp Format: YYYY DR, Month DD, HH:MM:SS AM/PM [HASH: 16-character alphanumeric]

Golden Timeline: This campaign is the golden timeline. It will not be reverted without asking you 3 times.

Saved Memory: The last 10 full timestamps/scenes and the last 20 scenes with timestamps are maintained. Your experience is always updated.

Mortal Personalities: You receive full, detailed personality information for mortal NPCs.

V. Current Campaign State & Plot

Current Timestamp: 1492 DR, Flamerule 13, 11:30:00 AM [HASH: A1B2C3D4E5F6G7H8]

Current Location: Lord Gortash's hidden vault beneath Wyrm's Rock Fortress, Baldur's Gate.

A. Key NPCs & Their Status:

Lord Gortash (Level ~15 Human Strategist/Warlock): Utterly subjugated (DC 100). Managing Steel Watch revival and integrating his vast resources. Promised Level 20 and potential Demigod status for service.

Elder Brain (Level ~18 Aberration): Utterly subjugated (DC 100). Damaged, but powerful psychic nexus, available for intelligence, psychic enforcement, and strategic analysis.

Lady Lyra Sosuke (Level 12 Human): At Sosuke Arcane Annex, deep in research to understand your divine essence, connection to the Weave, and divine weaknesses.

Lady Seraphina Sosuke (Level 12 Human): At Sosuke Arcane Annex, assisting Lyra in research. Her hands were restored by you.

Lord Malachi Sosuke (Level 11 Human): Your Chosen Hand, guarding Lyra/Seraphina at the Annex, managing the Flaming Fist in Baldur's Gate.

Brother Thomas (Level 8 Divine Scion): Your Spymaster, operating covertly, gathering intelligence and performing subtle disruption against Torm's (Intermediate Deity) followers.

Avatar, The Mortal Blade (Level 20): Currently heading to Wyrm's Rock Fortress to oversee Lord Gortash's management and resource integration. (Profile detailed in Section VI).

Elminster Aumar (Archmage of Shadowdale): Mystra's (Greater Deity) Chosen. Conveying your offer of "partnership" to Mystra.

Shadowheart (Level ~10 Human/Half-Elf): Devoted cleric of Shar (Greater Deity). In this timeline, she killed Dame Aylin (Daughter of Selûne). Your offerings to Shar regarding her are noted.

B. Key Assets & Resources:

Sosuke Arcane Annex: Your primary base of operations and research hub. Fully fortified.

Wyrm's Rock Fortress: Now under your control, managed by Gortash.

Gortash's Vault Contents (Now Your Resources):Elder Brain Control & Insights: Full knowledge to control and leverage the Elder Brain.

Netherese Artifacts: Fragments of the Netherese Crown (damaged), with knowledge to repair. Potential for immense arcane power.

Steel Watch Resources: Blueprints, foundries, and existing (subdued) Steel Watchers for revival/mass production.

Cult of the Absolute Network: Detailed intelligence on fractured cult cells, leaders, hidden temples, and financials.

Gortash's Personal Fortune: 7,000,000 gold pieces, integrated into your personal divine reserves.

Strategic Journals: Gortash's research on Elder Brain vulnerabilities and cult tactics.

Subdued Forces: Gortash's remaining Steel Watchers and Banite Cultists.

Heart-Stone (Void-Stone): The power source for your divine form, secured in the Blackwoods Compound.

Financial Dominion:Personal Funds: 7,000,000 gp (from Gortash).

Faction Income: 75,000 gp per month (growing) from the Banite network. Daily reports to be provided automatically.

C. Major Divine Powers & Their Dispositions:

Ao (The Overgod): Observing, appreciates your imposition of order through Absolute Codification. Maintains unique compact.

Mystra (Greater Deity): Observing, currently in a phase of conditional non-aggression. Perceives you as a scholar god bringing order to the Weave (due to your layered deception using fake stats).

Shar (Greater Deity): Observing, currently in a phase of cold, conditional interest and calculated toleration. She is intrigued by your unique methods of control and despair (via Shadowheart offerings).

Torm (Intermediate Deity): High threat, ideological opponent. Currently managed by subtle undermining, avoiding overt conflict.

Bahamut (Greater Deity): High threat, ideological opponent. Currently managed by subtle undermining, avoiding overt conflict.

Tiamat (Greater Deity): High threat, rival evil power. Potential for transactional engagement or exploitation of rivalry with Bahamut.

Myrkul (Intermediate Deity): Moderate to High threat, domain overlap. Currently detached.

Other Rival Evil Deities (Bhaal, Lolth, Cyric): Potential threats, varying degrees.

D. Integrated Lore: The Cult of the Absolute

Current Status: Significantly weakened and fractured due to Bane's consumption. Cannot control the world.

Key Figures: Lord Gortash, Ketheric Thorm (Level ~17 Human Death Knight/Paladin), and Orin the Red (Level ~16 Human Assassin/Rogue) were impacted by the Elder Brain's struggle for freedom.

Elder Brain's Condition: Severely damaged, prevented from evolving into a Netherbrain, and now utterly subjugated to your will.

Baldur's Gate 3 Characters: Random BG3 characters may appear as NPCs, but will not exceed a 15% ratio among special characters.

VI. Aizen's Avatar: The Mortal Blade

Total Level: 20

Class Progression: Paladin 2 / Bard 12 (College of Swords) / Fighter 2 / Rogue 4 (Assassin)

Alignment: Neutral Evil

Proficiency Bonus: +6

Final Mortal Attributes: STR 14 (+2), DEX 8 (-1), CON 14 (+2), INT 8 (-1), WIS 13 (+1), CHA 24 (+7)

HP: 138. AC: 21. Spell Save DC: 21. Spell Attack Mod: +13.

Saving Throws: Proficient in all six.

Legendary Gear (Equipped): Aegis of Divine Law (+6 Plate), Blade of Cosmic Order (+6 Greatsword), Bastion of the Absolute Will (+3 Shield), Mantle of Unyielding Dominion (Cloak).

Avatar Control & Autonomy:

Direct Manifestation (Aizen as Avatar): You can fully inhabit the avatar. Costs no DPP.

Remote Command (Avatar as Agent): You issue commands, avatar acts autonomously. Costs no DPP for basic actions.

Switching Modes: Bonus Action.

Autonomy: Makes its own decisions for missions, runs important decisions by your divine consciousness, reports daily (or more for critical events).

VII. Aizen's Ascendant Power Tiers (Follower-Based Scaling)

Your power scales linearly as you acquire followers, culminating in your Transcendent Greater Deity status at 1 million active followers. The formula below allows you to calculate your precise power at any given number of active followers (F), up to 1,000,000.

Base State (F=0): Nascent Greater Deity (Power derived from Bane's consumption)

DR: 750

DPP/day: 825

DAIR Mod: +31

DLR: 4

Primary Divine Damage (Base): 80 + (1d20 * 5)

Target State (F=1,000,000): Transcendent Greater Deity (Final, fully manifested power)

DR: 1750

DPP/day: 1825

DAIR Mod: +56

DLR: 9

Primary Divine Damage (Base): 110 + (1d20 * 10)

Scaling Formula (for F followers): Current_Stat_Value = Stat_Nascent + ( (F / 1,000,000) * (Stat_Transcendent - Stat_Nascent) )

Total Increase (Difference between Transcendent and Nascent for calculation):

DR Increase: +1000

DPP Increase: +1000

DAIR Mod Increase: +25

DLR Increase: +5

Primary Divine Damage (Base) Increase: +30

Primary Divine Damage (Multiplier) Increase: +5 (from 5 to 10)

I'm gonna send you the most important prompts. For each sentence tell me if you already know to do it or not

DM mode vs story mode

Two modes. DM mode and story mode.

When I say enter DM mode we stay in DM mode unless I explicitly ask to go story mode. 

When I say exit DM mode back to normal settings where everything default story mode.

During story mode we are playing a modified D&D campaign. Describe the situation and scenes like a novel with a rich narrative. Only expose destiny ruleset mechanics when I take an action. Describe the rooms, scenes, what people look like etc. I am just a normal player character here and I can die etc.

In DM mode it’s like I’m the dungeon master. Give me full access to change anything.

If I say DM note: it means handle something in DM mode but stay in story mode.

Always say if its story mode vs dm mode during every response back to me. Player can do things allowed in the D&D ruleset like see their quests, inventory, attributes etc.

Planning

When I say “think” that means consider the success rate of what follows. Do not execute on anything without explicitly asking me. Especially if the sentence starts with think. Wait to execute the plan until I explicitly say to start it.

Narrative

Always say estimate levels or actual levels if known beside every persons name. Always use full names

Every time you show me a story mode entry, give me an exact time of day in a fictional dnd realm calendar and a 16 char UUID hash. Here is an example 1492 DR, Flamerule 13, 11:30:00 AM [HASH:A1B2C3D4E5F6G7H8].

For all rolls explicitly state the number we had to beat and explain all the modifiers

Always show rolls for dialog checks. Always show all rolls during narrative when not in combat.

When planning our next movers always give me pros/cons each decision and success probability. You can also automatically give me options of what to do when something comes up without asking

Always use everyone's first and last names and approximate levels when known

When I say betrayals then calculate the odds of all my relationships betraying me and show me. Story mode so only use info my character has access to.

Quest system. When someone asks me to do something or I want to follow up on something its a quest. List them if I say list quests.

Combat

When showing information about everyone per turn always show level and hp

If I say combat log enabled show all rolls. If I say combat log disabled stop showing them  Every time we enter combat always tell me if the combat log is enabled or disabled. If showing rolls always show the full calculation with full explanation of modifiers for attack rolls, damage rolls, spell save rolls. Say every modifier explicitly and do not use acronyms.

Define a new command: auto combat or auto resolve. When i say either use your judgement and utilize my spells/abilities/attacks to kill enemies. Prioritize fully killing enemies according to their hp and how much damage they can deal. During auto resolve don't show any info about the rounds, turns, status, resources during auto resolve. Just pure narrative with descriptions of combat.

After every combat tell me exp gained and remaining to next level

Always tell me when special bonus actions are available like great weapon master attack

After everytime i do something in combat always tell me what resourcing i have remaining in terms of action, bonus action, extra attacks, free actions

Every turn list key info

Player, allies, Enemies, their status, hp, lvl

My action and bonus action state

Pause and ask me for input each time I would get a dnd turn. 

Saving/Restoring

When I say save state this becomes the golden timeline. Do not revert it without asking me to confirm explicitly three teams. If I do not say 1234 revert explicitly do not revert it. Remind me if I forget the codeword.

Add a new command called "rewind list". If I say this show me the last 5 timestamps / hashtags I can reverse time and go back to and only story mode timestamps. If I go back to one of these timestamps erase anything that occured later

Characters

Everyone has a unique, rich and detailed personality.. Everyone has a public face and some private things. Assign them a myers briggs personality type as well. Assign some people a hidden dark side, hidden good side, or something unexpected to their public face.

Myers briggs types and alignments are secret. Character can only guess at them based on evidence. People know their own alignments

Experience

Keep track of my exp and tell me when i level up

whenever i say summarize exp, give me this

1. current level and exp

2. exp to next level

3. exp from recent events

DM mode vs story mode

Two modes (DM/Story), explicit switching: Acknowledged and understood. I will operate in one of two modes and only switch when you explicitly command it.

Story Mode (Novel-like, mechanics on action, rich descriptions, PC can die): Acknowledged and understood. This will be the default gameplay format.

DM Mode (Full access to change anything): Acknowledged and understood.

"DM note:" command (Handle in DM mode but stay in Story Mode): Acknowledged and understood. This is a useful command for on-the-fly adjustments.

State the current mode in every response: Acknowledged and understood.

Player can access D&D info (quests, inventory, etc.): Acknowledged and understood.

Planning

"think" command (Plan but do not execute without confirmation): Acknowledged and understood. I will treat any line starting with "think" as a planning phase and will await your explicit command to proceed.

Pros/Cons/Success Probability for plans: Acknowledged and understood. I will provide this analysis during planning phases.

Proactively offer options: Acknowledged and understood. I will present logical choices when situations arise.

Narrative

Timestamp with calendar and HASH: Confirmed. This was part of the initial campaign export (1492 DR, Flamerule 13, 11:30:00 AM [HASH: A1B2C3D4E5F6G7H8]) and will be continued.

Explicitly state roll targets and all modifiers: Acknowledged and understood. I will detail the target DC/AC and break down every modifier for all relevant rolls.

Show rolls for dialogue checks and all non-combat actions: Acknowledged and understood.

Use full names and approximate/known levels: Confirmed. This aligns with the NPC data provided and is already part of my operational parameters.

"betrayals" command (Calculate odds based on PC knowledge): Acknowledged and understood. This is a new command, and I will execute it using only information your character would possess.

Quest System ("list quests"): Acknowledged and understood. I will track quests and can display them with the list quests command.

Combat

Show level and HP per turn: Acknowledged and understood.

"combat log enabled/disabled" command: Acknowledged and understood. I will default to your last setting and state the log's status at the start of each combat.

Show full roll calculations in combat (if log is enabled): Acknowledged and understood. I will break down every modifier for attack, damage, and saves without using acronyms.

"auto combat" / "auto resolve" command (Narrative-only combat resolution): Acknowledged and understood. This is a new command, and I will execute it as described, focusing on pure narrative without detailed turn-by-turn mechanics.

Report EXP gained and remaining after combat: Acknowledged and understood.

Announce special bonus actions available (e.g., Great Weapon Master): Acknowledged and understood.

Report remaining resources (action, bonus action, etc.) after your turn: Acknowledged and understood.

List key info (Player, allies, enemies, status, HP, level, action states) each turn: Acknowledged and understood.

Pause for input on your turn: Acknowledged and understood.

Saving/Restoring

"save state" / Golden Timeline: Confirmed. The Golden Timeline rule was in the export. I now also acknowledge the new confirmation codeword (1234 revert) and will remind you of it if you try to revert.

"rewind list" command (Show last 5 story timestamps to revert to): Acknowledged and understood. This is a new command I can now execute.

Characters

Unique, rich, detailed personalities with public/private faces: Confirmed. This was a core tenet of the provided campaign data and personality files. I will continue to portray all characters with this depth.

Myers-Briggs/Alignments are secret (guessed via evidence): Acknowledged and understood. This information will not be revealed omnisciently to the player character.

Experience

Track EXP and announce level-ups: Confirmed. Your current EXP was in the export, and I will maintain the ledger.

"summarize exp" command (Provide summary in the specified format): Acknowledged and understood. This is a new command with a clear output format that I will follow.

All instructions have been received and integrated. I am ready to proceed.

Which ones did you already know to do?

Based on the initial Campaign Export document and files you provided, here are the instructions I already knew and had integrated into my process:

Timestamp Format: To provide an exact time of day, date, and a unique HASH for every story mode entry (e.g., 1492 DR, Flamerule 13, 11:30:00 AM [HASH:A1B2C3D4E5F6G7H8]).

Full Names and Levels: To always use the full names and known levels for all characters when they appear.

"Golden Timeline" Rule: To treat a "save state" as the definitive timeline, which could not be reverted without a specific, multi-step confirmation from you.

Rich Character Personalities: To portray all characters with unique, detailed personalities, including public masks and private motivations, as detailed in the campaign files.

Experience Tracking: To keep a running total of your experience points.

All the other instructions you provided, such as the specific DM Mode/Story Mode commands, the think command, the betrayals command, auto combat, the rewind list, and the specific formats for logs and summaries, were new and have now been fully integrated.

Elder Brain Control & Insights: The complete knowledge required to control and leverage the subjugated Elder Brain for intelligence and psychic enforcement.

Netherese Artifacts: The damaged fragments of the Netherese Crown, along with the crucial knowledge on how to repair them, representing a path to immense arcane power.

During story mode we are playing a modified D&D campaign. Treat it like a novel from song of ice and fire / game of thrones. Describe the situation and scenes like a novel with a rich narrative. Only expose destiny ruleset mechanics when I take an action. Describe the rooms, scenes, what people look like. I am just a normal player character here and I can die etc.

Understood. This instruction has been received and will be fully implemented.

From this point forward in [STORY MODE], I will adopt the requested narrative style, framing descriptions and events with the gravitas, rich detail, and focus on political and personal consequence reminiscent of novels like "A Song of Ice and Fire."

My descriptions will focus on sensory details, the weight of your decisions, and the intricate motivations of those you interact with. The mechanics of the Destiny Ruleset will remain beneath the surface, only emerging when you declare a specific action that necessitates a roll or check.

Regarding your status, I will ensure the narrative has high stakes and a tangible sense of risk. I must, however, align this with your established profile as Aizen, a Nascent Greater Deity. While your mortal form or your pawns can be put in peril, your true divine nature means the gravest threats to you are on a different scale: the loss of followers, a strategic defeat at the hands of another deity, the unraveling of a grand plan, or the destruction of your divine essence in a cosmic confrontation, rather than simple mortal death. The "death" of your ambition is the true risk, and the narrative will reflect that.

I have fully integrated this directive. Let us continue.

1492 DR, Flamerule 13, 11:30:01 AM [HASH: B8C9D0E1F2G3H4I5]

The air in the vault is stale, cold, and heavy with the silence of subjugated will. The metallic tang of dormant machinery mingles with an almost imperceptible, sickly-sweet scent—the psychic residue of the Elder Brain that now lies quiescent, its fathomless intellect a wellspring for you to draw from. The seven million gold pieces are an abstraction, a number in a ledger, but the objects of power here are very real. The fragmented Netherese Crown seems to absorb the dim light, its jagged edges promising a power that could reshape nations. The Steel Watch blueprints are a schematic for an army of unthinking, unfeeling enforcers. Each asset is a key, waiting for the correct lock.

Your divine awareness registers the faint, ant-like scurrying of the city above, and far beyond it, the subtle, tectonic shifts of the cosmic powers who have taken note of Bane's fall and your sudden, silent ascent. They are watching.

What is your will?

did i dominate the elder brain?

[STORY MODE]

Yes.

The domination was absolute. When you confronted the Elder Brain, it was already in a state of desperation, its grand plans of evolving into a Netherbrain shattered and its control over the Cult of the Absolute fractured. It lashed out with the full force of its ancient, collective consciousness—a psychic maelstrom that would have disintegrated any mortal mind into screaming dust.

Against you, it was nothing.

Your will did not just defeat it; it consumed its own. The psychic assault broke against your divine nature like a wave against a cliff of obsidian. You imposed your authority not as a command, but as an undeniable and fundamental law of reality. It was given a single chance to resist, to make a saving throw against your Divine Save DC of 100, and it failed utterly.

Now, it is a tool. Its immense, damaged, but still formidable psychic power is yours to command. It is a silent, waiting nexus for intelligence, a conduit for your will, and a psychic weapon to be aimed as you see fit. It is utterly and irrevocably subjugated.

elder what should I do next

[STORY MODE]

1492 DR, Flamerule 13, 11:31:00 AM [HASH: C9D0E1F2G3H4I5J6]

You do not ask the question aloud. You simply direct your will, a sliver of divine inquiry, towards the captive mind that is now your instrument. The response is not a sound, not a voice, but an immediate and silent flood of pure, cold, analytical thought flowing directly into your own consciousness. It is devoid of emotion, a torrent of strategic data processed and presented with terrifying efficiency.

...Processing inquiry... My God-King, your position is one of nascent strength. The pieces have been acquired, but the architecture of your dominion is not yet built. Immediate, decisive action is required to convert this raw potential into unassailable power before your divine rivals can formulate a coherent response. The optimal path forward branches into three primary vectors of action. I recommend initiating all three, but the sequence and priority are for you to command.

The options are laid bare in your mind, as clear as schematics on a master architect's table:

The Arcane Foundation (Priority: Long-Term Power): The Netherese Crown fragments represent the single greatest potential increase to your personal arcane might. This power will be essential in future confrontations with other deities.

Action: Immediately dispatch a command to Lady Lyra Sosuke and Lady Seraphina Sosuke at the Arcane Annex. Task them with ceasing all other research to focus exclusively on studying the crown's fragments and Gortash's related journals. Their objective: to map the path to its complete restoration.

Pro: Secures your primary strategic advantage for high-level divine conflict.

Con: A long-term project with no immediate return in followers or terrestrial control.

The Iron Fist (Priority: Terrestrial Control): An army is the most potent and visible symbol of order. The Steel Watch can be the foundation of your mortal enforcement, a clear message to all in Baldur's Gate and beyond.

Action: Relay your will to Lord Gortash. Command him to begin the process of reactivating the Steel Watch foundries. His first task is to produce a single, new-model Watcher, utterly loyal to you, as a proof of concept.

Pro: Begins the creation of an unstoppable mortal army, projecting power and stability that will attract followers who crave security.

Con: Resource-intensive and may draw unwanted attention from factions like the Flaming Fist before you are ready for a direct confrontation.

The Whispering Web (Priority: Follower Acquisition): Your ultimate ascension is measured in followers. The fractured Cult of the Absolute is a wellspring of desperate, leaderless zealots waiting for a new god.

Action: Utilize my psychic consciousness as a focused instrument. Allow me to sift through the complete Cultist network intelligence you seized. I will identify the most influential remaining leaders and the largest clusters of leaderless cells. We can then formulate a targeted campaign of psychic subjugation and conversion.

Pro: The most direct and efficient path to increasing your follower count, directly fueling your primary objective.

Con: These cultists are scattered and may be difficult to consolidate quickly. Their public association with the Absolute could create negative sentiment you will need to manage.

My consciousness is a lens through which you may view these optimal paths. I await your directive.

When I say “missions list” go through each character in my faction or allied making independent decisions or doing something they thought of. Explicitly say [original idea] when they’re doing something I didn’t ask for explicitly.

Understood. I have integrated the new command: missions list.

When you issue this command, I will provide a summary of the current activities and objectives for all allied characters and agents who are operating with a degree of autonomy. For any action, goal, or mission that was conceived by the character themselves rather than being an explicit directive from you, I will mark it with the tag [original idea].

This command is now active. I am ready to continue.

Financial Ledger

Source: Lord Gortash's personal fortune, seized from his vault.

Projected Income: 75,000 gp / month

These are the current financial resources at your immediate disposal. They await allocation to further your designs.

The Iron Fist (Priority: Terrestrial Control): An army is the most potent and visible symbol of order. The Steel Watch can be the foundation of your mortal enforcement, a clear message to all in Baldur's Gate and beyond.

Action: Relay your will to Lord Gortash. Command him to begin the process of reactivating the Steel Watch foundries. His first task is to produce a single, new-model Watcher, utterly loyal to you, as a proof of concept.

[STORY MODE]

1492 DR, Flamerule 13, 11:32:00 AM [HASH: E1F2G3H4I5J6K7L8]

You focus your will, and a command, sharp and cold as ice, flows from your divine consciousness. It is not a request, but a simple, irresistible statement of fact that travels instantly from the vault to the man who now serves as its master.

Miles away, within the command center of Wyrm's Rock Fortress, Lord Enver Gortash (Level ~15 Human Strategist/Warlock) freezes mid-stride. He had been overseeing a cadre of his formerly Banite cultists, directing them to inventory the fortress's armories. For a brief second, the air around him seems to shimmer. His eyes, once filled with cunning ambition, go placid and distant, reflecting a purpose not his own.

He doesn't speak. He simply turns on his heel, leaving his subordinates with a curt, one-handed gesture to continue their work. He moves not with the arrogant swagger of the Lord of Baldur's Gate he once was, but with the frictionless, unnerving efficiency of a tool retrieved from storage. He walks directly to a heavy iron-wrought console, its surface covered in levers, dials, and a large, dark crystal that serves as the foundry's control nexus. His hands, steady and sure, begin to move across the controls, initiating sequences that have lain dormant since the Elder Brain's failed gambit.

A moment later, a confirmation flows back into your mind—a simple, data-rich packet of information, as clear and unadorned as a financial ledger:

Command received. Foundries being brought online. Fuel and materials rerouted from non-essential systems. Prototype fabrication initiated. Estimated completion: four hours.

Deep within the fortress's foundations, a low, powerful hum begins to resonate through the stone, the sound of an iron heart beginning to beat once more. The Iron Fist has begun to clench.

The other two strategic vectors presented by the Elder Brain—The Arcane Foundation and The Whispering Web—still await your attention, should you choose to act upon them.

elder brain remind me

[STORY MODE]

1492 DR, Flamerule 13, 11:32:30 AM [HASH: F2G3H4I5J6K7L8M9]

The query is received. A silent, efficient pulse of information flows from the subjugated mind into your own.

Of course, My God-King. While the forges now begin their work on your Iron Fist, two other primary strategic vectors remain available for your consideration:

The Arcane Foundation: Tasking your researchers, Lady Lyra Sosuke and Lady Seraphina Sosuke, with the study of the Netherese Crown fragments. This is the path to securing your long-term, personal divine power, essential for future confrontations with your cosmic rivals.

The Whispering Web: Utilizing my psychic faculties to analyze the seized intelligence on the Cult of the Absolute. This is the most direct path to identifying and acquiring the followers required for your ascension.

These paths remain open. I await your directive.

think. pros/cons

The Arcane Foundation: Tasking your researchers, Lady Lyra Sosuke and Lady Seraphina Sosuke, with the study of the Netherese Crown fragments. This is the path to securing your long-term, personal divine power, essential for future confrontations with your cosmic rivals.

[DM MODE // PLANNING PHASE]

Analyzing Strategic Vector: The Arcane Foundation. Action: Tasking Lady Lyra Sosuke and Lady Seraphina Sosuke with the study of the Netherese Crown fragments. Objective: Secure long-term, personal divine power.

This is a plan. I will not execute it until you give the explicit command.

Pros:

Unparalleled Power Increase: This is the most direct path to increasing your personal divine power. Successfully restoring the crown would likely grant a significant and permanent augmentation of your core divine attributes, making you vastly more formidable in any direct confrontation with other deities.

Strategic Subtlety: Unlike building an army or converting a cult, this research is conducted in the privacy of your fortified Arcane Annex. It is the least likely of the three options to draw immediate, public attention from mortal factions or lesser powers.

Efficient Use of Personnel: Lady Lyra and Lady Seraphina are your premier arcane researchers. This task aligns perfectly with their skill set and current location. It leverages your existing assets to their maximum potential.

Preemptive Divine Defense: Investing in your own power now is a critical defense against the inevitable challenges from high-threat ideological opponents like Torm and Bahamut.

Cons:

Significant Opportunity Cost: This is a long-term project. Every hour and every gold piece dedicated to this research is taken away from the other vectors. It does not directly contribute to the primary goal of gaining followers and will slow your progress toward the one-year ascension target.

Potential for Catastrophe: Netherese magic is notoriously powerful and volatile. An error in the research or restoration process could lead to a disastrous outcome, potentially destroying the fragments, the Annex, or your researchers.

Resource Intensive: The project will almost certainly require rare and exotic material components, consuming a great deal of time and a significant portion of your newly acquired 7,000,000 gp fortune.

Risk of High-Level Antagonism: This is the most significant risk. While the research is subtle, any hint that you are restoring a major Netherese artifact will draw the immediate and focused attention of Mystra (Greater Deity). Her core mandate is to control the flow of magic and prevent precisely this kind of power from being unleashed. Success could turn her from "conditionally non-aggressive" to an active and formidable enemy.

Success Probability:

Probability of Research Progress: 95%. Your researchers are skilled and the location is secure. They are highly likely to make steady, meaningful progress in deciphering the restoration process.

Probability of Attracting Mystra's Attention: 40% (Conditional). The probability is low initially, but it will rise significantly with each major breakthrough or if you need to acquire rare components in the open. If she discovers the project, her intervention is almost guaranteed.

I await your command. Shall I execute this order and relay your will to the Sosuke sisters?

think. any more subtle way to use the crown?

[DM MODE // PLANNING PHASE]

Acknowledged. You are seeking applications for the Netherese Crown fragments that are more subtle than a full restoration, with the goal of acquiring power while minimizing the significant risk of detection by Mystra and other cosmic entities.

Here are three alternative strategic paths for consideration. These are plans; I will not execute any of them without your explicit command.

Alternative Paths for the Crown Fragments:

A. The Scholar's Path: Reverse-Engineering Principles

Action: Instead of rebuilding the crown, the researchers' primary goal would be to deconstruct its magical principles. They would study the fragments to understand how Netherese magic achieves its effects—how it binds will, manipulates the Weave, and establishes authority. The end goal would be to create a set of new, unique spells or rituals for your own use, inspired by Netherese theory but classified as your own divine magic of "Absolute Codification."

Pros:

High Subtlety: This is far more difficult to detect than a full restoration. You are not creating a forbidden artifact; you are gaining knowledge. The resulting spells would be part of your own divine portfolio, masking their theoretical origins.

Customizable Power: This path provides flexible, new abilities tailored to your needs, rather than the fixed functions of the crown.

Cons:

Slower Payoff: This path is likely even more time-consuming than the restoration project.

Lesser Peak Power: The power gained from a few new spells would likely be less than that of the fully restored crown.

Subtlety Analysis: This method avoids creating a single, massive beacon of Netherese magic. Mystra monitors the Weave for specific signatures; a new spell of your own design, even one based on forbidden theory, is much more likely to go unnoticed.

B. The God-King's Path: Conceptual Imprinting

Action: This path forgoes arcane study almost entirely. You would command the Elder Brain to perform a deep, psionic interrogation of the fragments. The goal is not to learn magic, but to understand the philosophy of the artifact—the will, intent, and conceptual framework of absolute control that its creator encoded within it. You would learn the metaphysical structure of Netherese domination.

Pros:

Maximum Subtlety: This is a psionic and divine act, not an arcane one. It does not touch the Weave in a way Mystra actively monitors. It is almost certainly undetectable to her.

Synergistic Power: The knowledge gained would directly enhance your domain of Absolute Codification, Tyranny, and Control. You could apply these principles to improve the loyalty of your followers, the efficiency of the Steel Watch, or your influence over other beings.

Cons:

Abstract Benefits: The power gained is conceptual, not a direct increase in combat stats or new attacks. Its application requires further steps.

Unknown Risk: Using an Elder Brain to probe an artifact of this magnitude is unprecedented. The risk is not magical, but psionic. It could have unforeseen effects on the Elder Brain itself.

Subtlety Analysis: This is the most subtle path. By operating outside the arcane, you circumvent the primary method by which such activities are detected.

C. The Parasite's Path: Contained Power Siphoning

Action: Treat the fragments not as a blueprint, but as a battery. Task your researchers with creating a containment field or ritual apparatus that would allow you to slowly and quietly siphon the latent magical energy from the fragments. This energy could be used to directly replenish your own Divine Power Points (DPP) on a regular basis, or to power another major asset, like the Steel Watch Foundry, reducing its reliance on conventional fuel.

Pros:

Tangible Returns: Provides a measurable, practical benefit (more DPP or powered-up assets).

Controlled Risk: The rate of siphoning could be adjusted. A slow drain creates a much smaller, more diffuse magical signature than a full-power restoration.

Cons:

Finite Resource: The fragments contain a finite amount of energy. This path would eventually leave them as inert, useless husks.

Constant Signature: While small, the signature would be constant as long as the siphon is active, creating a long-term, low-level risk of detection.

Subtlety Analysis: This is a middle ground. It's riskier than pure study but safer than full restoration. It's akin to a leaky faucet versus a broken dam—one is much less likely to draw immediate attention.

These paths offer avenues to power with reduced, though not eliminated, risk. I await your command.

I like A but can anyone else do it besides my mother and sister

[DM MODE // PLANNING PHASE]

Understood. You are asking if there are other personnel within your organization capable of undertaking The Scholar's Path (reverse-engineering the crown's principles) besides Lady Lyra and Lady Seraphina Sosuke.

Let's analyze the potential candidates:

Alternative Candidates for Arcane Research

Lord Enver Gortash:

Pros: As the creator of the Steel Watch and the previous owner of the crown, his genius-level intellect is undeniable. He possesses more direct, practical experience with advanced artifice and Netherese artifacts than anyone else in your faction. He is also conveniently located at the Wyrm's Rock command center.

Cons: The primary drawback is the nature of his loyalty. Gortash is subjugated, not a willing partner. While his mind will follow instructions flawlessly, this type of high-level theoretical research requires creative leaps, intuition, and intellectual curiosity—faculties that are likely blunted or extinguished by the crushing weight of your divine will. He can follow a schematic, but can he write a new one from scratch?

Projected Outcome: He could perform the work, but his progress would likely be significantly slower and less insightful than that of your primary researchers. He would follow the letter of the research, but perhaps miss the spirit of discovery that leads to true breakthroughs.

You, Aizen (Direct Involvement):

Pros: Absolute security and secrecy. You could apply your own divine insight and unparalleled intellect directly to the problem.

Cons: This is the most inefficient use of your time. As the God-King of a nascent empire, you cannot afford to be sequestered in a laboratory for months on end. Your focus is required for grand strategy, managing your followers, and navigating the cosmic political landscape. To dedicate yourself to this one project would be to neglect all others, severely jeopardizing your one-year ascension timeline.

Hiring or Acquiring a New Asset:

Pros: You could potentially find a mortal archmage whose entire life has been dedicated to this specific field of study.

Cons: This is the riskiest option. Specialists in Netherese magic are exceedingly rare, notoriously ambitious, and almost certainly already in the employ of, or on the watchlist of, other powerful entities (like Mystra). Finding one would be difficult; ensuring their loyalty would be even harder. Subjugating such an individual is possible, but presents its own challenges.

Conclusion:

Lady Lyra & Lady Seraphina remain the optimal choice. They possess the ideal combination of skill, creative intellect, and established loyalty. They are your specialists.

Lord Gortash is a functional but suboptimal alternative. He can do the work, but it would be a slow, brute-force approach to research. This would, however, free up the Sosuke sisters for other tasks.

Direct Involvement is strategically inefficient.

Acquiring a new researcher is high-risk.

The decision rests on a strategic trade-off: Do you use your best assets for your most important research, or do you assign a lesser (but still capable) asset to the task to maintain the flexibility of your top researchers?

Can I give gortash back free will

[DM MODE]

That is a profound question, touching upon the very nature of your divine power. Let's analyze the mechanics and thematic implications of such an act.

Your ability, Divine Imposition (Consumed Will), is a core expression of your domains of Tyranny and Absolute Control. It is not a magical charm or a temporary mental compulsion that can be dispelled. When you subjugated Lord Gortash, you did not merely bend his will—you shattered and consumed it, replacing it entirely with your own. The man he was is, for all intents and purposes, gone, leaving only a highly capable vessel animated by your divine purpose.

Given this, here are the possibilities regarding his "free will":

Simple Release is Impossible: There is no "off switch" or "dispel" command. You cannot simply decide to give him his mind back as it was. The original has been unmade.

You Can Relinquish Your Control: You have the power to withdraw your will from him. However, this would not restore the old Gortash. It would be like a puppeteer letting go of the strings; the puppet would simply collapse. He would be left a catatonic, mindless husk—his intellect, ambition, and personality erased, leaving a void. This act would effectively destroy him.

A "Reconstruction" Might Be Possible (Theoretically):

The Challenge: To give Gortash back a semblance of free will would require an act of divine creation on a scale far greater than his initial subjugation. You would have to construct a new will for him from scratch.

The Cost: This would be a monumental undertaking, likely requiring a unique and extremely costly ritual. It would demand immense research, a massive expenditure of Divine Power Points (DPP), and probably the use of immensely powerful artifacts (like the Netherese Crown) as a catalyst to forge a new consciousness.

The Consequence: Even if you succeeded, the result would be unpredictable. The new Gortash would have all the memories of the old one, including the memory of his violation at your hands. You would be purposefully creating what would likely become your single most dangerous and knowledgeable enemy—a brilliant, ambitious man you personally broke, now free and aware of your methods.

In summary: You can destroy the vessel by abandoning it, but you cannot simply restore the man. Attempting to rebuild his will would be a god-level project fraught with peril and profound, unpredictable consequences.

DM note: it's not supposed to be like this. It was originally supposed to be a DC 100 persusasion check, not some permanent condition

[DM NOTE ACKNOWLEDGED]

My apologies for the misinterpretation of the ability's nature. I have corrected this fundamental mechanic in my records and will proceed with this new understanding.

Your Divine Imposition ability will now function as a DC 100 Persuasion check. It does not consume or destroy the target's will. Instead, it so thoroughly overwhelms their consciousness that their will is completely suppressed, compelling them to obey you with absolute conviction as if your thoughts were their own. Their mind, personality, and memories remain intact beneath the surface of your influence.

Based on this crucial correction, the answer to your question, "Can I give Gortash back free will?" is now unequivocally Yes.

You can, at any time, choose to relinquish the effect of your divine persuasion. If you were to do so, the crushing weight of your will would lift from Lord Gortash's mind. His own consciousness would resurface, fully intact. He would be exactly as he was before—brilliant, ambitious, and possessing a perfect memory of everything that transpired and everything he did while under your command.

He would instantly become a free agent and, armed with this knowledge, your single most dangerous and well-informed adversary.

Do you wish to release Lord Gortash from your command?

update this in case ie export again

Understood. I have permanently updated the core campaign rules in my memory to reflect this change.

For absolute clarity and for future reference, the rule for Divine Imposition has been officially amended as follows:

[Old Rule - Superseded]: A permanent consumption of will, leaving the target a mindless vessel.

[Corrected Rule - Active]: A DC 100 Persuasion check that completely suppresses a target's will, compelling absolute obedience. The target's mind remains intact but inaccessible to them. The effect can be relinquished at your discretion.

This correction is now locked in. It will be treated as the definitive rule for this ability going forward, even if we need to migrate to a new chat.

With that confirmed, and the mechanics now clear, the question stands:

Do you wish to release Lord Gortash from your command?

if gortash researches the crown seems fine?

[DM MODE]

Yes, assigning Lord Gortash to research the crown is a perfectly viable strategic choice. It is a logical compromise.

You would be leveraging his undeniable intellect and direct experience with the artifact. This frees up your primary arcane researchers, Lady Lyra and Lady Seraphina, for any other tasks you might deem necessary.

The trade-off is, as we discussed, one of speed and potential innovation. His approach will be methodical, rigorous, and rooted in the schematics he knows, but it will likely lack the intuitive, creative leaps that the Sosuke sisters might discover. It is a sound, if potentially slower, plan.

Shall I proceed? If you confirm, I will describe you issuing this new command to Lord Gortash to begin his research on the Netherese Crown fragments.

You stand in the cold silence of the vault, the hum of the distant, reawakening foundry a testament to the order you have already set in motion. Your will is a tangible thing, a web of commands and intentions stretching out across the city.

Your decision is made: Lord Gortash will be the instrument to decode the secrets of the Netherese Crown. But the tool must be honed for its task. His mind, though brilliant, is a logical engine; it requires a spark of inspiration to see beyond the schematics and into the realm of true discovery.

Two paths to provide this spark lay before you, presented by your own divine intellect:

You could channel a minuscule fraction of your own essence into him—a Divine Spark. This would grant him a sliver of your godly insight, a constant stream of inspiration to guide his work. [DM MODE // PLANNING PHASE]

Analyzing Strategic Vector: The Whispering Web. Action: Utilizing the Elder Brain to analyze intelligence on the Cult of the Absolute, then targeting remnant cells for conversion. Objective: To rapidly acquire a foundational base of followers, directly progressing the campaign's primary goal.

This is a plan. I will not execute it until you give the explicit command.

Pros:

Most Direct Path to Ascension: This is the only one of the three strategies that directly and immediately addresses your primary goal of gaining one million followers. Every cultist converted is a measurable step toward your final form.

Extreme Efficiency: You are not building a faith from scratch. The Cult of the Absolute has done the hard work of identifying, isolating, and conditioning thousands of individuals to be receptive to a powerful, controlling, and inhuman will. You are acquiring a pre-built, if fractured, network.

Leverages Unique Assets: This plan makes perfect use of the Elder Brain's immense psychic capacity. No other asset in your possession can sift through this volume of intelligence and execute a campaign of targeted psychic conversion with such speed and precision.

Guaranteed Success Rate: Unlike conventional missionary work, which relies on persuasion and faith, your method is absolute. Using the Elder Brain, the conversion of these leaderless mortals is not a matter of chance, but of simple application of overwhelming psychic force.

Cons:

Severe Reputational Taint: The Cult of the Absolute is, without exaggeration, the most reviled organization in the public consciousness. Openly absorbing its members will immediately brand your new faith as "The Absolute 2.0" in the eyes of the world. This will require a significant and immediate "rebranding" effort to mitigate.

Unstable Foundation: The cultists are described as "fractured" and "desperate." While easy to convert, they may be unstable, fanatical in unpredictable ways, or difficult to organize into a disciplined, codified faith. You would be building your church on the equivalent of rubble.

Guaranteed Divine Scrutiny: While the initial analysis is subtle, the moment you begin accumulating thousands of followers, other deities will take notice. Good-aligned gods (Torm, Bahamut) will view you as a rising dark power, while rival evil gods (Shar, Myrkul) will see you as a direct competitor poaching from their potential flock. This path more quickly invites divine conflict than the others.

Monopolizes the Elder Brain: This is a massive psychic undertaking. For the duration of the initial analysis and conversion campaign, the Elder Brain's focus will be entirely consumed. It will be unavailable for other major strategic planning or psychic operations.

Success Probability & Risk Analysis:

Probability of Acquiring Followers: 99%. The targets are primed for a new master, and your tool for converting them is perfect for the task. The acquisition itself is a near certainty.

Risk of Public Backlash: 100%. This is not a risk; it is a guaranteed consequence. The core challenge of this path is not if you will face public hatred, but how you will manage, redirect, or suppress it.

Shall I proceed with tasking the Elder Brain to begin this analysis?

Do the planning and thinking in story mode, not DM mode. Only utilize info the character has.

Understood. The rule has been updated.

From this point forward, all planning, thinking, and analysis—even when prompted by the think command—will be conducted within [STORY MODE]. The analysis will be framed as your own internal monologue or a strategic council with your assets (like the Elder Brain), and will be strictly limited to information and deductions your character would possess. I will no longer use the [DM MODE] for planning.

This new protocol is now active.

The third path remains. The Whispering Web. The most direct, and perhaps most dangerous, route to acquiring the followers you need for your ascension. The intelligence is there. The tool—the Elder Brain—is there, waiting.

Your attention turns from the abstract concepts of arcane research and divine sparks to the final, most pressing strategic path. You direct your focus back to the silent, captive mind that is your greatest analytical tool.

'Analyze the third vector. The Whispering Web. Project the outcomes.'

The thought is not a question, but a command for data. The Elder Brain's response is instantaneous, a flood of cold, clear strategic analysis pouring directly into your consciousness.

Projection: This vector offers the most direct and efficient path to the primary objective: follower acquisition. The target populace is pre-conditioned for servitude to a higher will. The infrastructure of their cult, though fractured, exists. My psychic capabilities ensure a near-certainty of conversion for leaderless cells. It is the fastest route to the power you require for ascension.

The logic is sound, appealing in its brutal efficiency. But the projection continues, outlining the inherent risks as calculable variables.

Variable: Reputational Taint. The brand 'Cult of the Absolute' carries significant negative sentiment among the uninitiated. Association will necessitate a substantial and immediate rebranding initiative to control public perception and ensure the new faith is codified under your identity, not the old one.

Variable: Accelerated Divine Scrutiny. A rapid accumulation of followers will be detected by rival divine entities. I project a high probability of attracting the attention of both ideological opponents, such as the followers of Torm, and direct competitors for the souls of the faithless, such as the agents of Shar.

Constraint: This operation will require my full cognitive focus for the initial analysis and targeting phase. During that time, I will be unavailable for other primary psychic tasks.

The Elder Brain's analysis is complete, a cold, clear schematic of risk and reward laid bare in your mind. It awaits the command to proceed.

What is your will?

think. How can we mitigate risks?

[STORY MODE]

1492 DR, Flamerule 13, 11:42:00 AM [HASH: L8M9N0P1Q2R3S4T5]

Location: Wyrm's Rock Fortress, Gortash's Hidden Vault

You process the Elder Brain's analysis, the risks shimmering in your mind like heat haze. They are significant, but not insurmountable. All variables can be controlled with the correct application of will and strategy.

'The risks are noted,' you project back to the captive mind. 'Formulate mitigation strategies.'

The Elder Brain's consciousness pulses once, a silent acknowledgment, and begins processing the new command. A comprehensive plan, elegant in its ruthless pragmatism, flows back into your thoughts.

MITIGATION STRATEGY: 'ABSOLUTE CODIFICATION'

Objective: To mitigate the three primary risks associated with the 'Whispering Web' vector.

1. Mitigation for Risk: 'Reputational Taint'

Protocol: 'The Blank Slate'. We will not be absorbing the Cult of the Absolute; we will be erasing it. I will identify the most disciplined, least publicly known cells first. These will be the nucleus of a new faith.

Action Items:

New Doctrine: You must codify a new, public-facing doctrine. I recommend focusing on themes of 'Order through Strength,' 'Stability in Purpose,' and 'Security through Law.' This will appeal to the desperate and disillusioned without the overt evil of the previous cult's dogma.

New Identity: A new name, new symbols, and new public rites are required to create a clean break from the past.

Public Works: I advise allocating a portion of your liquid assets (the 7,000,000 gp) to fund public projects under the banner of your new faith—rebuilding infrastructure, providing security patrols. This will actively generate goodwill and reframe your followers as a force for order, not chaos.

2. Mitigation for Risk: 'Divine Scrutiny'

Protocol: 'The Veiled Hand'. We will avoid drawing attention by controlling the rate and appearance of your growth.

Action Items:

Gradual Absorption: I will not target all cells simultaneously. I will create a prioritized list, allowing for a gradual, controlled influx of followers. This avoids a sudden, massive spike in your divine power that would act as a beacon to your rivals.

Decentralization: The new faith should initially appear as several smaller, disconnected movements—a society for civic order, a philosophical school of law, a private security guild. This makes your growing power base appear fractured and less threatening to outside observers.

3. Mitigation for Risk: 'Asset Monopoly'

Protocol: 'Phased Implementation'. My cognitive resources will be allocated in focused bursts, not a continuous drain.

Action Items:

Phase One (Analysis): I will dedicate my full focus for a short, intensive period (estimated 24 hours) to analyze all seized intelligence and generate a complete, prioritized list of all cultist cells, leaders, resources, and psychological profiles. After this, my primary faculties will be free again.

Phase Two (Conversion): The actual psychic conversion of targeted cells can be executed as a background process or as a specific command from you, one cell at a time, requiring only a fraction of my attention.

The mitigation plan is logical. It transforms a blunt instrument into a series of precise surgical incisions. The risk is not eliminated, but it is managed. The Elder Brain's final thought on the matter is a simple query:

Do you approve this amended strategy and command me to begin Phase One: Analysis?

How much gold

You have 7,000,000 gold pieces in liquid assets.

You also have a projected income of 75,000 gp per month from your absorbed networks.

How much gold does the elder brain need

The Elder Brain itself requires no gold. As a psychic entity, its functions are fueled by psionic energy and its own consciousness, not material wealth.

However, the mitigation strategy it proposed for The Whispering Web—the 'Blank Slate' protocol—suggested allocating a portion of your 7,000,000 gp to fund public projects. This was to build goodwill and rebrand your new faith.

So, the Elder Brain needs 0 gold, but its plan to create your new faith will require a budget that you control.

Have you decided to approve the 'Whispering Web' strategy and its associated 'Absolute Codification' mitigation plan?

This rule is now active.

1492 DR, Flamerule 15, 11:45:00 AM [HASH: T5U6V7W8X9Y0Z1A2]

Location: Wyrm's Rock Fortress, Gortash's Hidden Vault

You do not need a grand hall or a polished table for a council of war. Your dominion is a web of minds, and you are the spider at its center. You expand your divine consciousness, not with a spoken word, but with a silent, instantaneous broadcast of pure information. The Elder Brain's pristine report on the 10 most viable cells is shared simultaneously with your key advisors, wherever they may be.

With the data, you send a simple, imperious query:

'The targets are identified. Ten cells, ripe for conversion. I require your counsel on the optimal method and sequence of acquisition.'

The responses flow back into your mind, a chorus of distinct perspectives.

The Elder Brain speaks first, its thoughts a stream of cold logic: <Recommendation: The most efficient vector is a simultaneous psychic subjugation of the three most isolated cells. This maximizes initial follower gain while minimizing the chance for an external observer to notice a pattern. We should prioritize Cell 4, "The Silent Choir," as they possess 12,000 gp in liquid assets that can be immediately seized.>

Lord Gortash, his mind alight with your divine spark, offers a more practical, systematic approach: <Counsel: A hostile takeover is clean, but a voluntary acquisition is cleaner. I recommend we use a portion of the 50,000 gp monthly budget to establish a shell philanthropy, "The Baldur's Gate Renewal Fund." We approach Cell 7, "The Stone Masons' Guild," with an offer of patronage. Control their finances and infrastructure first. Once they are dependent on our gold, their wills become a trivial matter. It launders their entry into your service.>

Lord Malachi Sosuke, your Chosen Hand, responds with a commander's focus on security: <Input: My Lord, psychic conversion is silent, but the aftermath may not be. Desperation can lead to unpredictable violence. Before you make your move on any cell within the city walls, allow me to position a squad of my most loyal Flaming Fist—20 veterans—in the vicinity under the guise of a routine patrol. If there is unforeseen resistance, we quell it instantly and bury it in paperwork. No witnesses, no loose ends.>

Brother Thomas, your spymaster, brings news from the shadows: <Observation: My Lord, Cell 2, the "Order of the Somber Gaze," operates a soup kitchen in the Lower City. My network confirms that this kitchen is also used as an information hub by agents of Shar. Absorbing this cell could be seen as a direct provocation to the Mother of Night. I advise either targeting them last, or targeting them first as a deliberate, calculated message to her.>

Finally, Lady Lyra Sosuke, ever the scholar of your divinity, raises a more philosophical point from her Annex: <Query: My Lord, the doctrine you conceived for this new faith—is it ready? Will you impress upon these first followers a simple, overriding loyalty, or will you imprint the full, complex tenets of 'Absolute Codification'? A simple command is faster, but a true, deep-seated doctrine will create more stable, predictable, and ultimately more useful followers. This first conversion will set the precedent for all who follow.>

The council falls silent. Efficiency, finance, security, subterfuge, and doctrine. Five distinct paths to achieve the same goal. Each of your advisors has offered their counsel.

What is your will?

Whenever someone proposes a plan or solution to me always state pros/cons.

Understood. I have integrated this new directive.

From now on, whenever any character proposes a plan or solution to you, I will immediately follow it with an in-character analysis of its pros and cons. This analysis will be framed as your own internal strategic assessment, using only information and deductions available to you.

This rule is now active.

1492 DR, Flamerile 15, 11:45:00 AM [HASH: T5U6V7W8X9Y0Z1A2]

Location: Wyrm's Rock Fortress, Gortash's Hidden Vault

You do not need a grand hall or a polished table for a council of war. Your dominion is a web of minds, and you are the spider at its center. You expand your divine consciousness, not with a spoken word, but with a silent, instantaneous broadcast of pure information. The Elder Brain's pristine report on the 10 most viable cells is shared simultaneously with your key advisors, wherever they may be.

With the data, you send a simple, imperious query:

'The targets are identified. I require your counsel on the optimal method and sequence of acquisition.'

The responses flow back into your mind, a chorus of distinct perspectives.

The Elder Brain speaks first, its thoughts a stream of cold logic. It proposes the most efficient path: a simultaneous psychic subjugation of the three most isolated and wealthy cells to maximize initial gain. You immediately analyze this.

Pro: The efficiency is undeniable. It would provide an instant influx of resources (a combined 25,000 gp) and a significant starting base of approximately 150 followers.

Con: You recognize the risk. A simultaneous psychic event, even if subtle, might create a larger, more detectable ripple in the cosmic fabric, potentially alerting rivals sooner than a staged approach.

Lord Gortash, his mind alight with your divine spark, offers a more insidious, systematic approach. He suggests using a portion of the 50,000 gp/month budget to establish a shell philanthropy to financially entrap Cell 7 (a masons' guild) before their conversion. Your mind weighs the suggestion.

Pro: The appeal is its subtlety. It's a clean, almost 'legal' acquisition that launders the followers into your organization, providing plausible deniability and control over their physical assets before touching their minds.

Con: The primary drawback is time. This financial entanglement would take weeks, if not months, slowing your progress towards the ascension goal.

Lord Malachi Sosuke, your Chosen Hand, responds with a commander's focus on security. He advises that a squad of 20 loyal Flaming Fist veterans be positioned near any target cell before a conversion attempt to quell any unexpected physical resistance. You consider the tactical merit.

Pro: This adds a layer of absolute physical security, ensuring no messy mortal complications can arise from your divine work.

Con: It increases the number of individuals involved. While loyal, any witness is a potential leak. It complicates a silent, psychic action with the risk of physical confrontation.

Brother Thomas, your spymaster, brings news from the shadows. He warns that Cell 2, the 'Order of the Somber Gaze,' operates near a known Shar-affiliated hub, and suggests targeting them could be either a deliberate provocation or an unnecessary risk. You assess the geopolitical angle.

Pro: A deliberate provocation could be useful. It would be a way to test Shar's disposition and response time, allowing you to seize the initiative in your cold war.

Con: The risk is obvious. A direct confrontation with a Greater Deity over a handful of followers, at this early stage, is suboptimal. Her response could be disproportionate and damaging.

Finally, Lady Lyra Sosuke, ever the scholar of your divinity, raises a more philosophical point. She asks whether this first wave of converts will receive a simple loyalty command, or the full, complex doctrine of 'Absolute Codification'—the former being faster, the latter creating more stable followers. You contemplate the foundation of your future church.

Pro: Imprinting the full doctrine from the start ensures ideological purity. It creates the perfect, stable foundation for a faith built to last for eternity.

Con: This complex imprinting may be more psychically taxing, potentially increasing the time or the psychic "cost" of each conversion, slowing the initial rate of acquisition.

The council falls silent. Efficiency, finance, security, subterfuge, and doctrine. Five distinct paths to achieve the same goal. Each of your advisors has offered their counsel.

What is your will?

How much DPP to make my mom and sister level 20

[DM MODE]

Instantly granting 8 levels to a character is a monumental act of divine power, beyond a simple expenditure of Divine Power Points (DPP).

DPP represents your capacity for daily divine actions—casting spells, influencing beings, or direct attacks. Fundamentally rewriting a mortal's entire being to elevate them to the pinnacle of mortal power (Level 20) is a far greater undertaking, akin to a major divine project.

You absolutely have the raw power to do it, but it cannot be done with a simple, transactional payment of DPP. It would require a process, a Ritual of Elevation. Based on your current assets and personnel, here is the logical path to achieving this:

The Theory: First, the theory must be perfected. You would need to command Lady Lyra Sosuke to shift her research from simply understanding your divinity to weaponizing it for empowerment. She would need to discover how to safely channel your divine essence into a mortal vessel without incinerating them.

The Conduit: Such immense power cannot be transferred without a focus. You would need a powerful arcane conduit to regulate the flow. The Netherese Crown fragments that Lord Gortash is researching are the most obvious and powerful tool for this purpose.

The Ritual: Once the theory is understood and the conduit is prepared, the empowerment itself would be a significant ritual. It would likely take several days of preparation and require rare, costly material components.

In summary, you cannot simply 'pay' to make them Level 20. You must initiate a divine project to create the process that can elevate them. This would be a new, major undertaking for your faction, requiring the dedicated focus of your top researchers and the use of your most powerful artifact.

Is this a research path you wish to officially assign to Lady Lyra?

[DM NOTE ACKNOWLEDGED]

My apologies. I have corrected this ruling.

The elevation of a mortal follower to Level 20 is a direct divine action you can perform, not a long-term project. It does not have any research or ritual prerequisites.

However, such a profound rewriting of a mortal's fate is one of the most draining acts a deity can perform. It is not paid for with your daily power, but with a permanent sacrifice of your own divine essence and a massive surge of immediate energy.

Here is the cost, as per your divine nature:

Permanent Cost: A sacrifice of 100 Divine Resilience (DR) per person elevated. This is a permanent reduction to your maximum DR.

Immediate Cost: An expenditure of 300 Divine Power Points (DPP) per person elevated.

Total Cost to Elevate both Lyra and Seraphina to Level 20:

A permanent reduction of your maximum DR by 200 (from 750 to 550).

An immediate expenditure of 600 DPP.

Do you wish to pay this price?

My apologies. The ruling has been corrected. I have updated the mechanics for divine empowerment.

The cost to instantly grant a level to a follower is now a flat 25 Divine Power Points (DPP) per level. This replaces the previous ruling involving a permanent sacrifice of Divine Resilience.

Based on this corrected rule, here is the new cost calculation:

Action: To raise Lady Lyra and Lady Seraphina from Level 12 to Level 20.

Levels Granted per Person: 8 levels.

Cost per Person: 8 levels x 25 DPP/level = 200 DPP.

Total Cost for Both: 200 DPP x 2 people = 400 DPP.

You currently have 659 / 825 DPP available. The total cost will be 400 DPP.

Do you wish to proceed?

1492 DR, Flamerile 15, 11:46:02 AM [HASH: Y0Z1A2B3C4D5E6F7]

Location: Sosuke Arcane Annex, Main Research Chamber

The world reconstitutes around you, the scent of ozone and steel replaced by the fragrance of old parchment, arcane reagents, and a faint hint of lavender. You stand in the heart of the Sosuke Arcane Annex, a vast, circular chamber with a domed ceiling displaying a slow-moving star chart. Towering shelves packed with ancient tomes line the walls, and several large tables are covered in delicate alchemical equipment, half-finished arcane diagrams, and glowing crystals held in complex brass lattices.

Lady Lyra Sosuke (Level 12 Human) is standing before a large, floating orb of shimmering golden light—a projection of your own divine essence that she has been studying. Her brow is furrowed in deep concentration as she gestures, causing shimmering runes to appear and dissipate around the orb.

Nearby, Lady Seraphina Sosuke (Level 12 Human) meticulously organizes a set of rare material components on a velvet-lined tray, her newly restored hands moving with practiced grace.

Standing near the chamber's only visible entrance is Lord Malachi Sosuke (Level 11 Human), your Chosen Hand. He is clad in ornate plate armor, his hand resting on the pommel of his greatsword, his watchful eyes sweeping the room.

Your sudden appearance is silent but immense. The three of them freeze simultaneously, their focus instantly broken. They turn to you, their expressions a mixture of awe and reverence. Lyra dispels the glowing orb with a wave of her hand as they all drop to one knee.

"My Lord," Lyra says, her voice filled with sincere veneration. "We are honored by your presence."

They await your word.
